Title: Gen AI Trainee Program
Description:
Country: Portugal

COMEÇA AQUI

O Santander (www.santander.com) está a evoluir de uma marca global de grande impacto para uma organização orientada pela tecnologia, e as nossas pessoas estão no centro desta jornada.

Juntos, estamos a liderar uma transformação centrada no cliente, que valoriza o pensamento crítico, a inovação e a coragem de desafiar o que é possível.

Esta é mais do que uma mudança estratégica. É uma oportunidade para pessoas motivadas crescerem, aprenderem e fazerem uma diferença real.

A nossa missão é contribuir para que mais pessoas e empresas prosperem. Adotámos uma cultura de risco sólida, e todas as nossas pessoas, em todos os níveis, têm um papel ativo e responsável na gestão de riscos.

O TEU IMPACTO

O Santander está à procura de um(a) estagiário/a de GenAI para Lisboa.

Procuramos talento com interesse em desenvolvimento de software e inteligência artificial para integrar a nossa equipa de GenAI. O foco será o desenvolvimento de aplicações e microserviços que utilizam modelos de AI generativa pré-treinados (como os da OpenAI) para resolver desafios reais de negócio. Esta é uma excelente oportunidade para aplicar conhecimentos técnicos em projetos inovadores com impacto direto.

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:


Desenvolver aplicações e microserviços que integrem modelos de GenAI via APIs.
Criar e manter pipelines de dados e serviços backend escaláveis.
Trabalhar com equipas multidisciplinares para identificar casos de uso e implementar soluções baseadas em IA.
Garantir boas práticas de engenharia de software, incluindo testes, versionamento e integração contínua.
Explorar novas ferramentas e abordagens para melhorar a eficiência e a qualidade das soluções desenvolvidas.


O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

FORMAÇÃO ACADÉMICA


Formação em áreas de STEM. (obrigatório)


IDIOMAS


Português (obrigatório)
Inglês (preferencial)
Espanhol (preferencial)


Hard Skills


Conhecimentos em linguagens de programação como Python, SQL e Java. (obrigatório)
Experiência com bibliotecas e frameworks de IA. (preferencial)
Containers (Docker, Kubernetes). (preferencial)
Prompt Engineering. (preferencial)
OpenAI (preferencial)
RAG (preferencial)
Experiência com ferramentas de versionamento de código (Git). (preferencial)
Conhecimento em computação cloud (Azure, Aws). (preferencial)
Conhecimento com ferramentas de CI/CD como GitHub Actions, GOCD ou similares. (preferencial)


Soft Skills


Atitude proativa e dinâmica.
Capacidade de trabalho em equipa e de relacionamento interpessoal.


VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.


Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.


Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas.

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Pronto(a) para dar o próximo passo na tua jornada?

Esta é uma excelente oportunidade para aplicar conhecimentos técnicos em projetos inovadores com impacto direto.

Estamos a redefinir a forma como trabalhamos através da inovação, da tecnologia de ponta, da colaboração e da liberdade para explorar novas ideias.

Para ter sucesso nesta função, serás responsável por:

  • Desenvolver aplicações e microserviços que integrem modelos de GenAI via APIs.
  • Criar e manter pipelines de dados e serviços backend escaláveis.
  • Trabalhar com equipas multidisciplinares para identificar casos de uso e implementar soluções baseadas em IA.
  • Garantir boas práticas de engenharia de software, incluindo testes, versionamento e integração contínua.
  • Explorar novas ferramentas e abordagens para melhorar a eficiência e a qualidade das soluções desenvolvidas.

O TEU CONTRIBUTO

As nossas pessoas são a nossa maior força. Cada indivíduo contribui com perspetivas únicas que nos tornam mais fortes como equipa e como organização.

Capacitamos as equipas para irem mais além, valorizando quem são e potenciando o que trazem consigo.

Os seguintes requisitos representam os conhecimentos, competências e capacidades essenciais para o sucesso nesta função. Serão consideradas adaptações razoáveis para permitir que pessoas com deficiência possam desempenhar as funções essenciais.

FORMAÇÃO ACADÉMICA

  • Formação em áreas de STEM. (obrigatório)

IDIOMAS

  • Português (obrigatório)
  • Inglês (preferencial)
  • Espanhol (preferencial)

Hard Skills

  • Conhecimentos em linguagens de programação como Python, SQL e Java. (obrigatório)
  • Experiência com bibliotecas e frameworks de IA. (preferencial)
  • Containers (Docker, Kubernetes). (preferencial)
  • Prompt Engineering. (preferencial)
  • OpenAI (preferencial)
  • RAG (preferencial)
  • Experiência com ferramentas de versionamento de código (Git). (preferencial)
  • Conhecimento em computação cloud (Azure, Aws). (preferencial)
  • Conhecimento com ferramentas de CI/CD como GitHub Actions, GOCD ou similares. (preferencial)

Soft Skills

  • Atitude proativa e dinâmica.
  • Capacidade de trabalho em equipa e de relacionamento interpessoal.

VALORIZAMOS O TEU IMPACTO

A tua contribuição é importante e será reconhecida. Podes esperar um pacote de remuneração justo e competitivo, que reflete o impacto que crias e o valor que entregas.

Mas sabemos que as recompensas vão muito além dos números.

  • Oferecemos mais do que um salário. Os nossos benefícios foram concebidos para apoiar a tua vida, os teus objetivos e o teu bem-estar, agora e no futuro.
  • A tua saúde é a nossa prioridade. Através do BeHealthy, o nosso programa global de bem-estar, promovemos o bem-estar holístico.
  • Sabemos que a família é tudo. Por isso, oferecemos licença parental, apoio à infância e programas adaptados a cada fase da vida.
  • Potenciamos as nossas equipas através de oportunidades globais e percursos de carreira diversificados.
  • Acreditamos no poder da ajuda. Pode dedicar parte do teu tempo de trabalho a atividades de voluntariado.

Estamos aqui para te manter motivado(a), ajudar-te a alcançar os teus objetivos e celebrar o teu progresso em cada etapa do caminho.

CONFORMIDADE LOCAL

O Santander orgulha-se de ser uma organização que promove a igualdade de oportunidades, independentemente da idade, género, deficiência, estado civil, raça, religião ou orientação sexual.

Comprometemo-nos a oferecer um processo de candidatura inclusivo e acessível a todas as pessoas.

O QUE FAZER A SEGUIR

Se consideras que esta função é do teu interesse, candidata-te.

Pronto(a) para dar o próximo passo na tua jornada?

Title: Data Visualization Engineer
HTML:
Excelia is a multinational consulting, technology, and professional services firm with over 25 years of experience. We provide services in more than 50 countries across Europe, Latin America, and the United States from our 10 offices.

Do you have experience in the field of Data Visualization and are you looking for a new challenge? Then this opportunity is for you!

🔹 Position: Data Visualization Engineer

📍 Location: Hybrid - Lisbon or Porto

Main responsibilities:

  • Collaborate with data analysts and business stakeholders to understand data requirements and objectives for visualization projects;
  • Connect and integrate visualization tools with various data sources, ensuring seamless and efficient data access;
  • Extract, prepare, and cleanse data from various sources, ensuring data quality, integrity, and accuracy for visualization purposes;
  • Design and develop visually engaging and insightful data visualizations using appropriate tools and techniques.

Required Qualifications:

  • BSc and/or MSc in Computer Science or similar;
  • Previous experience with Data Visualization, Business Intelligence, and ETL processes;
  • Proficiency in data visualization tools such as SAP Analytics Cloud, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik, or similar;
  • Proficiency in data prep tools or components such as Tableau Prep, Power Query, Alteryx, or similar;
  • Solid understanding of data extraction, preparation, and cleansing techniques to ensure data quality and integrity;
  • Experience with database management systems, SQL, and various data formats, such as CSV, JSON, and XML;
  • Ability to connect and integrate visualization tools with various data sources, such as APIs, databases, or data lakes.

If you think you’re a good fit, don’t hesitate to apply for this opportunity.😉

Title: Product Analyst - ComparaJá
Description:
About us

Our mission is to help consumers save time and money by matching them with the best products for their banking, insurance, and utility needs. We work towards becoming the largest distributor of financial services. ComparaJá.pt is part of Samlino Group (https://samlinogroup.com/). We operate across three markets, empowering users reach healthier financial lives.

What we offer you

You'll have the unique opportunity to be a key driver of our exponential growth curve. In your daily work, you will work directly with our Founder and senior teams.

Sunny Lisbon will be your home base, but you'll travel between our three markets (Portugal, Denmark and Greece)!

Your Responsibilities

You will be accountable for your own projects and will be responsible for driving results across various ventures and initiatives. Your responsibilities in this job will evolve and be defined and redefined throughout your time with us. The aim is to take ownership of and drive the development of progressively larger opportunities.

What we expect from you

We are looking for a dynamic individual who thrives in a results-oriented environment to join our team and help us reach our objectives. Industry experience is not a requirement, but you do need to be highly ambitious, proactive, forward-thinking, a fast learner, and driven to succeed.

Furthermore, we are looking for candidates who:


Have a proactive mindset, strong analytical skills, and the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams
Are self-motivated, detail-oriented, and possess a track record of successfully owning projects from conception to completion
Possess an unwavering drive and desire to be the very best at what they do
Are passionate about making an impact and driving tangible results
Exude a strong work ethic fueled by an urgency to progress
Embrace ownership and proactively follow through on commitments
Possess an insatiable curiosity about business, data, and people, relishing the opportunity to build and nurture relationships that fuel lasting success
Master communication, wielding words with impact both verbally and in writing
Showcase unrivalled organisational skills, skillfully structuring and prioritising their work to achieve maximum efficiency
Have full proficiency in either Portuguese or Danish and fluent in English
EU work permit is required


Start date and Location

Flexible start date

This role is full-time onsite at our Lisbon office

Title: Senior Business intelligence Specialist
Description:
At Efficio, we're not just a consulting firm; we're a team of dedicated experts committed to transforming procurement and supply chain functions. Our mission is to drive sustainable, measurable value for our clients through innovative solutions and deep industry insights. Join us and be part of a dynamic environment where your skills and ideas make a real impact.

Efficio is the world’s largest specialist procurement and supply chain consultancy, with offices across Europe, North America, and the Middle East. We’re a diverse team of over 1,000 individuals, representing more than 60 different nationalities and speaking 40+ languages – and we’re continuing to grow rapidly!

We are recruiting for our Lisbon Office where you'll be joining a growing team of nearly 100 people. Consultants based in Lisbon will support clients globally with the opportunity for international travel. Our consultants provide flexible support to clients whether it is a rapid assessment, short term savings realisation or embedding in a long-term partnership to deliver lasting and measurable value to clients.

This role is ideal for an experienced BI professional who can work independently with senior stakeholders, translate ambiguous business questions into robust reporting solutions, and lead the delivery of high-quality dashboards and analytics products. The successful candidate will bring strong technical experience across BI tooling, SQL, databases, data modelling, and visual analytics, combined with the communication skills needed to influence stakeholders and drive better decision-making through data.

The primary BI platform used in this role will be Sigma, so hands-on Sigma experience is highly desirable. Candidates with strong experience in comparable BI platforms such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or Qlik, and the ability to quickly adapt to Sigma, may also be suitable.

There is also an opportunity to explore the use of n8n, AI agents, and other automation or agentic AI solutions to enhance reporting workflows, generate insights from dashboards, automate recurring analysis, and improve how stakeholders interact with data.

What we are looking for:


5–8 years of experience in business intelligence, analytics, reporting, or data visualization, with strong hands-on experience using Sigma. Experience in Power BI, Tableau, Looker, can be considered.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases, data models, joins, KPIs, calculations, and reporting logic.
Proven ability to translate ambiguous business requirements into clear, practical BI solutions, dashboards, and actionable insights.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, including experience working with senior technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, improve existing reporting processes, and maintain a strong focus on data accuracy, quality, and usability.


Nice to Have:


Experience with n8n, AI agents, workflow automation, or agentic AI solutions.
Familiarity with Python for data analysis, automation, validation, or prototyping.


We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people!




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.




As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Analyst!




Responsibilities:

Responsible for creating data pipelines in Airflow for data extracting, processing and loading
Responsible for code and query optimization
Responsible for generating insights and identify trends, anomalies or opportunities to improve users experience and anticipate their daily tasks
Responsible for delivering data in useful and appealing ways to users.
Responsible for understanding business change and requirement needs, assess the impact and the cost.




Requirements:

Python experience.
Experience in creating APIs in Python
Power BI experience.
PySpark experience.
SQL, Oracle Data Base experience.
Experience with Data Warehouses experience.
Apache Airflow - Experience in pipeline development.
Git experience.
Agile methodologies.
Good level of english.







Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




What you can expect from us?

Long-term projects with national and international context.
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally.
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust.
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com with reference “CA/DataAnalyst”.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!

Your next challenge might start here. 😄




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Decskill was founded in 2014 as an IT Consulting Company and their main mission is to delivery value through the knowledge. We enable companies to meet the chalenges of digital world by providing our clients with business models that ensure technological capacity, flexibility and agility. We are more than 500 consultants with offices in Lisbon, Porto and Madrid.




DECSKILL operates in 3 main areas:




DECSKILL TALENT, through which we provide our clients with an extension to their IT teams;




DECSKILL BOOST, through which we provide our client with software development models to increase capacity and optimize Time-to-Market, where we create and manage teams that deliver according to their needs, at the desired speed;




DECSKILL CONNECT through which we provide our client with consulting services, as well as the implementation and management of information technology infrastructures.




Our practice results in the creation of value for our customers, either by delivering qualified and value-added services, or through highly qualified and motivated professionals, as well as technology solutions that allow us to operate and transform the business of our customers.




We are looking for a Data Analyst:




RESPONSABILITIES:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team.
Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals.
It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




TECHNICAL SKILLS:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas)
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus




IDIOMAS:

Inglês - Avançado- (Obrigatório)
Francês - Noções - PLUS




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to carolina.mustra@decskill.com with reference “CM/Data Analyst”




Thank You! 😊




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being

Experience as a Data Analyst

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Imagine working for a company where your growth is monitored every day, even from a distance. Where you are not just another number, but part of a constantly expanding team.

We are more than 2430 Primers spread across the world, and we want one more: YOU.




We are looking for a Data Analyst:




Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines using Apache Airflow for data extraction, processing, and loading (ETL/ELT processes)
Optimize code performance and database queries to ensure efficiency and scalability
Analyze data to generate insights, identify trends, detect anomalies, and uncover opportunities to enhance user experience and anticipate user needs
Deliver data in clear, useful, and visually appealing formats for stakeholders
Understand business requirements, assess impacts, and estimate implementation costs
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support data-driven decision-making




Technical Skills

Strong proficiency in Python
Experience in building APIs using Python
Experience with PySpark
Advanced knowledge of SQL and Oracle Database
Hands-on experience with Data Warehousing concepts and tools
Proven experience with Apache Airflow (pipeline development and orchestration)
Version control experience with Git
Familiarity with Agile methodologies




Location: Lisbon | Porto

Project Type: National and/or international

Level of Experience: Mid (more than 4y)

Salary Package & Benefits: €1,200 to €2,000 (salary base) + Food Allowance

+ Flex Benefits

If you are ready for the next step in your career, send your CV to jobs@primeit.pt and join PrimeIT!

Title: Data Analyst Trainee
Description:
About The Job


The Transaction Banking business concerns two fundamental business lines:
Cash Management: all products that assist our customers with their liquidity needs (current accounts, term deposits, payments & collections, forex)
Global Trade Solutions: all products that assist our customers with their global trade needs (letters of credit, guarantees, supply chain management, factoring)
As Transaction Banking Chief Operating Officer (TB COO) team, we assist our different business team in multiple topics, both on daily/monthly needs as in project mode. Within the COO team we have a small team of data scientists focusing on data, who are involved in the data use cases from exploration phase up to production delivery.
This position provides an excellent opportunity to learn about the overall banking industry as a whole, more specifically dealing with the Transaction Banking business across all regions (EMEA, APAC, AMER)
The Data Analyst Trainee’s main mission is to provide support to the Cash Management fee analysis Unit and to contribute to the pricing to billing initiatives

Your Main Activities Are


Automate business dashboards as prototypes and prepare them to be put in production, working closely with the team´s data analysts
Report to Top line TB Management and TB COO on all data queries and data needs
Contribute to the development and governance of our data and support the different Cash Management business line with data-driven decision-making
Meet different stakeholders to frame their data needs, based on your findings collect the necessary data, process the data, and turn it into meaningful tools meeting the requirements expressed by the teams
Participate and interact with inter-disciplinary teams such as IT, Business and Operations and contribute to the improvement and streamlining of data systems within the company to drive innovation


Profile And Skills To Success


Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Management, Business Intelligence, Business, Economics or Mathematics
Fluency in English
Highly proficient with MS Excel
Ability to quickly comprehend new software tools and learn how to use them optimally
Great attention to detail
Excellent interpersonal, communication and influence skills
Good conceptualization skills and data exposure
Open, curious and eager to learn and discover day to day banking products


Why joining BNP Paribas?


Leading banking institution


Leading player in banking and financial services in Europe, BNP Paribas operates in 64 countries and has nearly 178,000 employees, including more than 144,000 in Europe


International reach


The Group holds key positions in several areas of banking and financial services that are structured around three operating divisions:


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB) connects companies needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities division that offers tailored financial solutions for corporate and institutional clients.
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS) brings together all our commercial banks and several specialised businesses
Investment & Protection Services (IPS) combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real estate including specialised businesses offering a wide range of savings, investment and protection services
Our presence in Portugal


In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.220 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 centres of expertise providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.


Diversity and Inclusion commitment


BNP Paribas ´ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility – Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.


Remote Working Conditions


At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.


Commitment towards work/life balance


Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Business Data Analyst
HTML:
Company Description

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 27,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.4 billion euros in 2024.

Job Description

Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights? At Inetum, we are looking for a Business Data Analyst to bridge the gap between data and business, ensuring that information is available to support strategic decisions efficiently.

Key Responsibilities

  • Gather and define business requirements for data initiatives;
  • Extract and analyze data from commercial and analytical systems for ad hoc analysis;
  • Design and structure data models to create valuable products and ensure data consistency;
  • Perform business analysis to support operations and decision-making;
  • Communicate effectively with stakeholders and translate insights into actionable recommendations;
  • Contribute to the evolution of our Data Warehouse and Datahub.

Deliverables

  • Development of dashboards and data visualization strategies;
  • Creation of documents and reports to support decision-making;
  • Compilation of information and preparation of analytical reports;
  • User Stories for Data Warehouse enhancements;
  • Data modeling within the Data Warehouse;
  • Ad hoc data collection from commercial and analytical systems.

Qualifications

  • Up to 10 years of experience in similar roles;
  • Advanced knowledge of SQL for data extraction and manipulation;
  • Strong experience with dashboards and data visualization tools;
  • Advanced Excel and proficiency in Office tools;
  • Preferably experience with SAS Enterprise Guide and DOMO;
  • Solid understanding of data modeling and warehousing concepts.

Title: Data Analyst – GCP
Description:
CAPGEMINI ENGINEERING

At Capgemini Engineering, the world leader in engineering services, we bring together a global team of engineers, scientists, and architects to help the world’s most innovative companies unleash their potential. From autonomous cars to life‑saving robots, our digital and software technology experts think outside the box as they provide unique R&D and engineering services across all industries. Join us for a career full of opportunities.

Where you can make a difference. Where no two days are the same.




YOUR ROLE

Key Responsibilities

Take ownership of data analysis initiatives, demonstrating a proactive, detail-oriented, and results-driven approach
Analyze, transform, and interpret complex datasets to generate actionable insights and support data-driven business decisions
Collaborate closely with business stakeholders and cross-functional teams to gather requirements and translate them into analytical solutions
Design, build, and maintain efficient queries, data models, and datasets using GCP tools, with a strong focus on BigQuery
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, patterns, anomalies, and opportunities
Ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability through validation processes, monitoring, documentation, and dataset labeling
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and KPIs, enabling business users to monitor performance and make informed decisions
Use cloud-based and big data technologies to process and analyze large volumes of structured and semi-structured data
Communicate insights, findings, and recommendations clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Promote a data-driven culture by supporting best practices in analytics, reporting, and data governance




YOUR PROFILE




Experience & Technical Skills

4–6 years of proven experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytics role
Strong proficiency in SQL, with hands-on experience querying large datasets
Experience with Python for data analysis and data preparation (e.g., Pandas, PySpark or equivalent)
Solid hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform, especially BigQuery
Strong experience building and maintaining interactive dashboards in Power BI, enabling effective data storytelling
Good understanding of data modeling, KPIs, and business metrics
Knowledge of basic statistical concepts (descriptive statistics, trend analysis, hypothesis testing) applied to business analysis
Experience working in large-scale, cloud-based data environments and data warehouses
Familiarity with version control tools (e.g., Git) and data pipelines / CI/CD concepts is a plus
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to explain complex insights clearly and concisely
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills
Academic background in Engineering, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related field
Mandatory fluency in English




WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WORKING HERE

Join a multicultural and inclusive team environment
Enjoy a supportive atmosphere promoting work‑life balance
Hybrid work
Your career growth is central to our mission. Our array of career growth programs and diverse professionals are crafted to support you in exploring a world of opportunities
Access valuable training and certifications in cutting‑edge technologies
Engage in exciting national and international projectsHealth and life insurance
Referral program with bonuses for talent recommendations
Great office locations

Capgemini is an AI‑powered global business and technology transformation partner, delivering tangible business value. We imagine the future of organizations and make it real with AI, technology, and people. With our strong heritage of nearly 60 years, we are a responsible and diverse group of 420,000 team members in more than 50 countries. We deliver end‑to‑end services and solutions with our deep industry expertise and strong partner ecosystem, leveraging our capabilities across strategy, technology, design, engineering, and business operations.

Title: Data & Business Intelligence
Description:
ABOUT US:

At Go4Mobility, we are your trusted partner for Digital Mobile Solutions. As a leading technology company, we specialize in multichannel digital communication and messaging services, helping businesses enhance interactions with their customers through seamless and innovative solutions.

RESPONSIBILITIES:


Work closely with cross-functional stakeholders to translate business requirements and needs (e.g., reporting) into data solutions.
Develop and maintain data integration processes (ETL/ELT).
Design data models and ensure efficient data organization (e.g., star schema, data marts).
Integrate data from multiple sources (databases, APIs, internal/external systems).
Ensure data quality, consistency, availability, and governance.
Define and evolve data architecture.
Develop reports (semantic layer/model) and metrics (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) to support decision-making.
Identify trends and provide insights and recommendations for improvement.
Collaborate with technical and business teams to enhance and democratize data usage.
Document data models, processes, and developed dashboards/reports.
Promote a data-driven culture within the organization.



QUALIFICATIONS:


Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics or a related field.
Experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or similar roles.
Strong knowledge of Excel and SQL.
Experience with ETL processes / data pipelines (data processing, transformation, and analysis).
Experience with Python or a similar language (data processing) – nice to have
Experience in data modeling and data warehousing.
Experience with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
Experience with Cloud technologies (Azure / GCP).
Knowledge of data modeling principles.
Understanding of performance, scalability, and tuning.
Strong ability to communicate with non-technical stakeholders
Results-oriented mindset (not only technical), with the ability to translate data into clear insights
Collaborative mindset, working effectively between IT and business teams
Fluent in English, both written and spoken.
Availability to work in a hybrid model (2-3 days a week at the Lisbon office).



What We Offer


A Welcoming Workplace: Enjoy our newly renovated office in Alcântara, designed for a dynamic, friendly, and multidisciplinary team.
Work-Life Balance: We offer a hybrid and flexible work model, respecting both your professional and personal time.
Professional Development: We are committed to helping you grow and achieve your goals. With our Training Plan, you will find opportunities to enhance your professional skills while pursuing your personal ambitions.
Wellness Activities.
Extra time off: Throughout the summer months, every employee gets to enjoy a well-deserved Friday afternoon off each month. It’s our way of helping you make the most of the sunny season!

  • Collaborative mindset, working effectively between IT and business teams
  • Fluent in English, both written and spoken.
  • Availability to work in a hybrid model (2-3 days a week at the Lisbon office).


What We Offer

  • A Welcoming Workplace: Enjoy our newly renovated office in Alcântara, designed for a dynamic, friendly, and multidisciplinary team.
  • Work-Life Balance: We offer a hybrid and flexible work model, respecting both your professional and personal time.
  • Professional Development: We are committed to helping you grow and achieve your goals. With our Training Plan, you will find opportunities to enhance your professional skills while pursuing your personal ambitions.
  • Wellness Activities.
  • Extra time off: Throughout the summer months, every employee gets to enjoy a well-deserved Friday afternoon off each month. It’s our way of helping you make the most of the sunny season!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Marley Spoon

We’re Marley Spoon, a food-tech company on a mission to make amazing, home-cooked meals easy and sustainable. Since 2014, we’ve grown into a global meal kit platform, delivering flexible, personalized recipes to customers across six countries.

Behind every box is a network of intelligent systems, built by lean, cross-functional teams who care deeply about customer experience, sustainability and quality. Our Data Tribe plays a key role in making that happen – one insight, one experiment, and one decision at a time.

About The Role

As a Data Analyst, you’ll turn complex customer and growth data into clear insights that guide decisions across Marketing, Product, and Commercial teams. You’ll design analyses, build dashboards, and support experiments that help us understand what drives acquisition, conversion, retention, and long-term customer value.

You’ll have the chance to go beyond reporting: working on high-impact business questions, evaluating performance trends, and helping stakeholders understand what is truly moving the needle. This is a hands-on role with real ownership, where your work will be seen, used, and acted on.

What You’ll Do

In this role, you will:


Design, analyze, and interpret A/B tests and quasi-experiments to inform marketing and growth decisions.
Build and maintain reliable dashboards and data models in Looker to support self-serve analytics across the business.
Explore customer behaviour across acquisition, retention, engagement, and lifecycle performance using data from Snowflake, Amplitude, and other sources.
Investigate changes in growth, conversion, and commercial performance to identify what is driving impact.
Work closely with Marketers, Product Managers, and cross-functional stakeholders to frame business questions, choose the right analytical approach, and communicate results clearly.
Share findings in a simple, visual way so stakeholders can act quickly and confidently.


About You

You’re curious, analytical, and commercially aware. You enjoy turning open questions into structured analysis, and you care about making your work understandable and actionable.

Must-haves


Around 3+ years of experience in data analytics, ideally in marketing, growth, product, e-commerce, or a similar business environment.
Confidence with SQL, including joining tables, building aggregations, and writing efficient queries.
Experience with BI / data visualisation tools (Looker preferred, but similar tools are fine).
Hands-on experience working with data warehouses (e.g. Snowflake or similar).
Practical experience with experimentation (A/B tests and/or quasi-experimental methods), either designing, analysing, or interpreting tests.
Clear communication skills – you can explain complex concepts in a simple, visual, and structured way.
Comfortable working in English in a distributed team.


Nice-to-haves


Familiarity with Python or similar tools for deeper analysis.
Experience with Amplitude or other product analytics tools.
Previous experience in subscription, marketplace, DTC, or other data-rich consumer businesses.
Experience with cohort analysis, lifecycle analytics, funnel performance, or behavioural segmentation.


If you don’t meet every single point but feel you can grow into this role, we’d still be happy to hear from you.

How We Work – Location, Remote & Collaboration


This role is remote within Portugal – we can hire you anywhere in PT, as long as you have the right to work here.
We have a Lisbon office where you’re welcome to join collaboration days, workshops or social events, but regular office attendance is optional.
We collaborate mainly during European business hours, with flexibility to organise your day in a way that works for you and your team.


Benefits


Remote with optional office days.
22 annual leave days +2 days extra days for every year of tenure (up to 6).
5 training days per year.
Private health insurance provided by Tranquilidade.
Food allowance of 7.62€/worked day under by Coverflex.
24/7 confidential employee assistance program.

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
About Us

GoCardless is a global bank payment company. Over 100,000 businesses, from start-ups to household names, use GoCardless to collect and send payments through direct debit, real-time payments and open banking.

GoCardless processes US$130bn+ of payments annually, across 30+ countries; helping customers collect and send both recurring and one-off payments, without the chasing, stress or expensive fees. We use AI-powered solutions to improve payment success and reduce fraud. And, with open banking connectivity to over 2,500 banks, we help our customers make faster, more informed decisions.

We are headquartered in the UK with offices in London and Leeds, and additional locations in Australia, France, Ireland, Latvia, Portugal and the United States.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

Payment Intelligence

Our mission is to make GoCardless the best way to pay and be paid. Our ambition is to dramatically improve payment performance and safeguard our customers from bad outcomes like payment fraud and failures. You will help find data opportunities and areas to improve our payment intelligence products. You should be comfortable with large data sets, customer data, and machine learning principles. You’ll work alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Data Scientists in our two core Payment Intelligence teams to provide critical insights and help drive product direction.

Our existing Payment Intelligence Products are already one of the key reasons why customers choose GoCardless. These include Protect+, which defends customers from fraud, and Success+, which helps customers intelligently recover failed payments. You will need to own our understanding of these products and identify insights and opportunities to help us develop new intelligent features.

What You'll Do

At GoCardless analytics is at the core of everything we do and drives business performance through data insight. You will play a critical role partnering closely with stakeholders across the business (including product, operations, marketing, and commercial teams) in order to deliver actionable insights to influence decisions at every level of the organisation. The role is focussed on identifying value and driving change throughout the business derived from insights. Your insights will be turned into actions that will help drive the future of GoCardless. You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.


Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)


What Excites You


Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.


What Excites Us


You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!


Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff


Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)


Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here as well as our latest D&I Report

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. In 2021 we became co-founders of the Tech Zero coalition, a group of businesses committed to taking climate action as part of the UNFCCC Race to Zero. We aim to reduce our impact and to create positive change on the natural world. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

If you are looking for a dynamic, innovative, and technology-driven company, Inetum is the place for you! Come be Inetum!

Key Responsibilities

Maintain and develop Python scripts for data processing and transformation
Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards, using tabular models
Ensure the quality, consistency, and accessibility of delivered data

Good knowledge of Python, particularly for automation and data processing
Experience with ETL pipelines and data modeling
High level of autonomy and sense of responsibility
Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
Good communication skills and ability to work with multiple stakeholders
Results-oriented mindset with a focus on continuous improvement

Location: Areeiro, Lisboa

  • Experience with ETL pipelines and data modeling
  • High level of autonomy and sense of responsibility
  • Strong analytical skills and attention to detail
  • Good communication skills and ability to work with multiple stakeholders
  • Results-oriented mindset with a focus on continuous improvement

Informação adicional

  • Workplace type: Hybrid (max. of 3 times per week in the office)
  • Location: Areeiro, Lisboa

Title: Data and Reporting Analyst Trainee
Description:
About The Job


A Data/Reporting Analyst Trainee supports the management of Group Learning & Development (L&D) team, in all topics requiring transversal coordination or consolidation of information. The trainee provides support to management processes, such as planning and/or monitoring the execution of any type of activity, such as a project or a process, being accountable for a wide variety of tasks requiring transversal coordination or consolidation and impacting the business


Your Main Activities Are


Understand the needs expressed by HR teams and the business
Contribute to monitor and analyse of HR data by creating KPIs, updating and creating reports/dashboards
Assess and ensure control to monitor the data quality
Develop, maintain, and update standard operating procedures (SOPs) for data handling, reporting, and dashboard governance
Clearly communicate actionable recommendations orally and in writing through reliable and quality deliverable


Profile And Skills To Success


Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Management or Economics
Knowledge in Tableau and Power BI
Knowledge in Microsoft Office Pack (namely Excel, PowerPoint, and Teams)
Knowledge of Data Quality
Fluent in English, both spoken and written
Teamwork and collaboration
Good communication skills
Good organizational skills
Rigor and attention to detail
Analytical ability
Problem-solving skills
Able to deliver and results driven


Why joining BNP Paribas?

BNP Paribas, The European Leader In Banking And Financial Services In Europe, Operates In 64 Countries And Has Nearly 180,000 Employees. The Group Is Structured Around Three Operating Divisions. Its Business Lines Cooperate Closely, As Part Of Our Integrated Model, To Meet The Needs Of All Our Clients In a Coordinated Manner


Corporate & Institutional Banking (CIB), connects corporate clients needing financing and institutional clients looking for investment opportunities;
Commercial, Personal Banking & Services (CPBS), brings together all the Group’s commercial & personal banking and several specialised businesses;
Investment & Protection Services (IPS), combines a unique continuum of products and services across protection, saving, investment and real-estate.


Our presence in Portugal

In Portugal since 1985, BNP Paribas today has more than 9.700 employees, distributed across the Group's 10 business entities established in the country. Its presence also extends to 11 excellence centres providing value-added services to various countries where the BNP Paribas Group also operates.

Diversity and Inclusion Commitment

BNP Paribas´ Ambition Is To Cultivate An Open And Responsive Environment For All That Encourages Collaboration And Interaction. We Demonstrate Our Commitment By Actively Contributing To The Group’s Diversity And Inclusion Strategy And Goals, In Line With The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals. As Part Of Our Local Corporate And Social Responsibility - Diversity & Inclusion Strategy, BNP Paribas Portugal Is Committed To


Promote a fair and unbiased recruitment process and offer professional development opportunities to all employees;
Celebrate diversity and advocate for inclusion, both externally and internally, encouraging employee’s participation, creating space for different voices to be heard.


To foster the effort of BNP Paribas Portugal, multiple initiatives and events take place throughout the year where our people can find out more on the importance of diversity inclusion at the workplace and in our society.

Remote Working Conditions

At BNP Paribas, we embrace a Smart Working framework based on trust, autonomy and collaboration. Within this framework, eligible employees can benefit from flexible remote working options adapted to our hybrid working environment. To ensure a comfortable and efficient working setup, eligible employees are provided with both office and home equipment, are entitled to an equipment allowance and can benefit from exclusive partnerships to purchase additional items at reduced prices.

Commitment towards work/life balance

Supporting employees in a hybrid way of working while providing them with the means to maintain a work-life balance is an essential dimension of our Smart Working program. At BNP Paribas, we care about our employees’ wellbeing and promote a culture of good integration between work and personal life.

To find out more on why you should join BNP Paribas please read our Employee Value Proposition and our Career path page.


Please note that only applications submitted in English will be considered.
In case you are selected for this role, further documentation will be requested to support your hiring process.

Title: Business Intelligence Consultant (Power BI)
Description:
Descrição da empresa

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. Inetum’s team of 28,000 consultants and specialists strive every day to make a digital impact for businesses, public sector entities and society. Inetum’s solutions aim at contributing to its clients’ performance and innovation as well as the common good. Present in 19 countries with a dense network of sites, Inetum partners with major software publishers to meet the challenges of digital transformation with proximity and flexibility. Driven by its ambition for growth and scale, Inetum generated sales of 2.5 billion euros in 2023.

Descrição do emprego

📌 About the Role

We are looking for a Business Intelligence Consultant (Power BI) with proven experience in implementing BI solutions. This professional will be responsible for transforming data into actionable insights, collaborating with cross-functional teams, and supporting strategic decision-making for our clients.

🛠️ Responsibilities


Design and develop interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI
Participate in business requirements analysis and stakeholder engagement
Model data and build efficient, scalable BI solutions
Ensure data quality, consistency, and security across all deliverables
Collaborate with technical and functional teams to deliver value to the client


Qualificações

🎯 Technical Requirements


Minimum of 3 years of experience in BI projects using Power BI
Strong command of DAX, Power Query, and data modeling
Experience with relational databases (e.g., SQL Server, Azure SQL)
Knowledge of Azure Data Services (e.g., Data Factory, Synapse) is a plus
Microsoft certifications are preferred (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate and/or Microsoft Fabric)
Fluency in Portuguese and a good level of technical English


🌟 Valued Soft Skills


Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Analytical thinking and problem-solving orientation
Proactive and able to work independently
Adaptability to different contexts and client environments
Team spirit and collaborative mindset


Informação adicional

🎁 What We Offer


Involvement in challenging and innovative projects
Opportunities for professional growth and career development
Access to continuous training and Microsoft certifications
A collaborative, results-driven work environment

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
In 1966, in Marseille, Pierre Bellon laid the foundations of what would become Sodexo, guided by a simple yet powerful idea: improving everyday life through services that truly matter.

Today, Sodexo is a global leader in Food and Services, supporting people at every moment of life, at work, in schools, hospitals and communities. With 426,000 colleagues across 43 countries, we serve 80 million consumers every day, generating €24.1 billion in revenues. Our growth goes hand in hand with social and environmental responsibility, driven by our purpose: to create a better everyday for everyone and build a better life for all.




About Sodexo Business Services (SBS)




Portugal is no exception! Seven years ago, we established Sodexo Business Services (SBS), our shared service center. Here, you will have the opportunity to join various Financial teams such as Record to Report, Order to Cash, Purchase to Pay, Financial Analysis, and Master Data, supporting European markets and ensuring business growth.




Join Us!

We are looking for talented professionals to be part of our team, bringing unique skills and perspectives to help us grow even further.




Data Analyst

The Data Analyst is responsible for the technical execution of reporting and insight that enhances transparency & business performance with particular emphasis on digitization (Power BI), process efficiency & solution




⚙What will you do?

Enhancing our digital reporting suite & ensuring smooth execution of business-critical activities.
Demonstrate strong technical capability across reporting tools,
Accountable for scoping, designing, and delivering reporting developments.
Explore new ways to leverage reporting technologies, with a focus on building advanced proficiency in tools such as Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Ensure that reporting output and architecture is coherent and consistent with our governance framework and standards.
Evaluate reporting processes and recommend improvements to enhance efficiency and reduce non‑value‑adding analysis.
Engage proactively with stakeholders to deliver reporting and insights, building effective business partnerships and communicating data in a clear, relatable narrative.
Translate broad or unclear stakeholder requirements into structured reporting plans and deliverables.
Be able to shift between data modelling, content creation, commercial insight & business partnering.




🤙What You Need to Succeed:

Bachelor’s degree in relevant field
Fluency in English required, ability to speak other European language is a plus
Focus on produt excellence, integrity and costumer focus
Experiency in Power BI, Power Automate, and Excel.
Continuous improvement mindset
Communication skills
Problem solving
Be flexible and versatile




What we have to offer you?

Hybrid working model;
Flexible working hours;
Health & Life Insurance;
Meal allowance paid in Meal Card;
Additional Days off: extra vacation day, employee’s birthday, volunteering day;
Opportunity to grow professionally inside the Company;
Possibility to participate in multicultural projects;
Several internal activities aiming to promote our team´s wellbeing.




📩 Apply now! Send your CV to Recruitment.SBS.Portugal@sodexo.com with the job title in the subject line. Explore all our career opportunities here: Sodexo Careers.

Learn more about Sodexo: YouTube - Sodexo.




Diversity & Inclusion Statement

Sodexo is committed to creating a diverse and inclusive work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment based on merit.




🔒 Data Privacy:

We respect your privacy. Your personal data will be used exclusively for recruitment purposes, processed under GDPR regulations, and treated confidentially. For further details, contact privacy.sbs@sodexo.com.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.

As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We are looking for an Data Analyst!




Responsabilities:

As a Data Analyst, you will play a vital role in our team. Working directly with one or more of the bank's business areas, you will provide valuable insights to drive business growth, understading their needs, proposing and implementing data-driven solutions (analysis, visualization and automation). You will exercise a lot of problem identification and problem solving skills.
You will have the opportunity to take ownership of business essential analytical projects that involve retrieving, preparing and analysing complex datasets using data/statistical models and best practices in data visualization for the reporting creation, with the goal to produce insights and automated tools that will guide strategic solutions
You will move between technical and non-technical areas and should be able to communicate well with different types of professionals. It is very important to be able to clearly and concisely understand stekeholders’ needs and translate these to our technical team, as well as present the results of complex analysis to our stakeholders.




Requirements:

Academic background in Engineering, Economics, Computing, Mathematics, Statistics or related fields.
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI.
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas).
Professional experience in any data visualization tool such as Tableau or Power BI.
Professional experience in data analysis with programming, preferably Python (Pandas).
Ability to analysze, synthetize and simplify.
Knowledge of descriptive statistics and hypothesis testing is a plus.
Knowledge in Big Data technology such as Pyspark, Presto and Airflow is a plus.
Knowledge of Power Query and DAX is a plus.
Good level of English.




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

Long-term projects with national and international context
Opportunities to grow technically and professionally
A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust
Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability




If you’re interested in this job, please send your CV to cesaltina.abreu@decskill.com with reference “CA/DataAnalyst".




Your next challenge might start here.

Join us! 😊




Decskill is committed to equality and non-discrimination with all our talents. We recruit and promote talent, based on diversity and inclusion, regardless of age, gender, ethnicity, race, nationality or any other form of discrimination incompatible with the dignity of the human being.

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

OPERATIONS
The Operations team serves as the backbone of our marketplace, seamlessly managing the entire order journey. From creating online content and ensuring secure payments to order fulfillment and delivery, while providing comprehensive customer and partner care, this team is relentlessly focused on improving the customer experience every single day.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
We are looking for a highly analytical and business-oriented Data Analyst to join our Data Analytics, Automation & AI team. In this role, you will transform complex datasets into actionable insights, build scalable analytics solutions and contribute to AI-enabled initiatives that support strategic and tactical decision-making.

The ideal candidate has strong expertise in Python, modern analytics practices and hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) data products. The candidate should be comfortable working with large datasets, designing reporting frameworks, automating workflows and communicating insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Key Responsibilities

  • Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, opportunities and business insights using Google BigQuery;
  • Develop scalable data pipelines, automated reporting solutions and analytical models using Python and Airflow;
  • Work with GCP services, primarily BigQuery and Looker, to build and optimize analytics workflows;
  • Design and maintain dashboards, KPIs and self-service reporting solutions for business stakeholders;
  • Translate business requirements into analytical solutions and actionable recommendations;
  • Collaborate with engineering, product and operations teams to support data-driven decision-making;
  • Perform data validation, quality checks and root-cause analysis to ensure data integrity;
  • Optimize SQL queries and improve performance of large-scale data transformations;
  • Support automation and AI-driven initiatives by leveraging modern analytics and data engineering practices;
  • Document analytical processes, data models and reporting frameworks to ensure maintainability and scalability.




Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering or a related field;
  • 3+ years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence or analytics engineering roles;
  • Advanced proficiency in Python for data analysis, automation and workflow development;
  • Strong SQL skills and experience working with large-scale relational and analytical databases;
  • Hands-on experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP), particularly BigQuery and Looker;
  • Strong understanding of data modeling, ETL/ELT processes and analytics engineering concepts;
  • Experience working with orchestration and automation tools such as Airflow;
  • Experience working with version control systems such as Git;
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to present insights to technical and non-technical audiences;
  • English proficiency is mandatory;
  • Curious mindset, proactive attitude and strong collaboration skills.




Preferred / Nice To Have Qualifications

  • Exposure to Data Science and Machine Learning concepts, including predictive modeling and statistical analysis;
  • Familiarity with modern AI such as Natural Language Processing (NLP), Large Language Models (LLMs) and deep learning frameworks;
  • Knowledge of machine learning libraries and frameworks such as Scikit-learn, TensorFlow or PyTorch;
  • Experience working in Agile environments and using tools such as Jira and Confluence.




Rewards & Benefits

  • Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools;
  • Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
  • Training opportunities;
  • Flexible benefits program




EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT

  • Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process.
  • We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.




SCAM DISCLAIMER

Please be aware that Farfetch does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Title: Business Data Analyst Trainee (f/m)
Description:
O Grupo Lactogal afirma-se como um grupo de pessoas para pessoas, unido por um propósito comum: Cuidar da origem e alimentar o futuro.

Temos uma oportunidade para fazeres parte deste grupo através da empresa Lactogal.

Poderás integrar uma equipa dinâmica, comprometida com o ambiente e com as comunidades onde está inserida e focada no bem alimentar.

Candidata-te!

Business Data Analyst Trainee para reforçar a equipa de Análise de Negócio na Sede – Porto.

RESPONSABILIDADES


Apoiar a equipa na análise de desempenho do negócio, através da preparação de análises e suporte a simulações comerciais e financeiras
Colaborar na produção e análise de relatórios de atividade comercial (vendas, KPIs, mercado e concorrência), contribuindo para a disponibilização de informação de suporte à decisão
Participar no acompanhamento do orçamento de vendas e indicadores comerciais, com base na análise de dados históricos e evolução do negócio
Acompanhar a monitorização de vendas e performance comercial, contribuindo para a identificação de desvios e oportunidades de melhoria
Apoiar o desenvolvimento de análises de rentabilidade e a avaliação de iniciativas comerciais e promocionais, contribuindo para a otimização dos resultados



REQUISITOS


Licenciatura no âmbito da Gestão, Economia, Engenharia de Dados
Conhecimento na utilização de Excel



O QUE OFERECEMOS


Remuneração ajustada à função e às qualificações
Contrato de estágio com duração de 1 ano

Title: Data Analyst - Porto
Description:
Quadrante is looking to strengthen its team by hiring a Data Analyst for our Porto office.




As a leading engineering and consultancy group with a strong international footprint, Quadrante delivers high-value and innovative solutions across multiple markets. With more than 1.400 professionals worldwide, we are driven by excellence, collaboration, and a strong commitment to the highest professional and ethical standards.




In this role, you will contribute to transforming data into meaningful insights, supporting decision-making processes, and enhancing business performance across the organization.







Responsibilities:

Collect, process, and analyze data across different areas of the Group.
Assess data quality and ensure accuracy, consistency, and reliability of information.
Develop reports and dashboards to support business decision-making.
Continuously improve analytical models and data analysis processes.
Provide data-driven support to different business units, solving problems through data modeling and analytical techniques.




Profile:

Degree in Information Management, Information Systems, Computer Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related field.
Up to 3 years of experience in similar roles.
Strong experience in SQL, Python, or equivalent programming languages (mandatory).
Experience with Business Intelligence tools (mandatory).
Advanced knowledge of English (mandatory).
Knowledge of Spanish will be valued.
Strong analytical thinking, communication skills, and team spirit.




What we offer

As part of the Quadrante Group, you will join an ambitious and collaborative team within a growing international organization.

We work every day to deliver high-quality solutions and foster the growth of our people through engaging challenges and diverse projects.

A dynamic and inclusive work environment, where knowledge sharing and teamwork are key.
The opportunity to work with diverse datasets and support multiple business areas.
A role with real impact, contributing directly to strategic decision-making.
A culture that values potential, continuous learning, and professional development.




Join the Quadrante Team!
