Title: Data Analyst (Product)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Analysts and Scientists ‌work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a talented Data Analyst to dig into our complex databases, look for root causes of a problem, design your own solutions, and write your own code to implement them. We aim for you to never stop learning, picking up new skills and delivering value along the way.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You’ll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through our data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Maintaining and creating new dashboards to track metrics and visualise insights
Promoting data literacy across the company, organising and holding workshops


What You'll Need


Previous experience in an analytical role, creating impactful solutions
A background/education in a quantitative discipline
5+ years of experience with SQL
Great skills with Python or other programming languages
Evidence of solid mathematical and statistics knowledge


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 Procuras o próximo passo como Data Analyst?




Então este desafio pode ser para ti.

Estamos à procura de um(a) Data Analyst com forte perfil analítico e orientação para negócio.




🧑 💻 Como será o teu dia a dia:

Analisar datasets grandes e complexos para identificar tendências, oportunidades e insights de negócio
Desenvolver pipelines de dados escaláveis, reporting automatizado e modelos analíticos com Python e Airflow
Trabalhar com serviços GCP, sobretudo BigQuery e Looker, para construir e otimizar workflows analíticos
Desenhar e manter dashboards e KPIs para stakeholders de negócio
Traduzir requisitos de negócio em soluções analíticas e recomendações acionáveis
Colaborar com equipas de engenharia, produto e operações para suportar a tomada de decisão
Fazer validação de dados, quality checks e root-cause analysis
Otimizar queries SQL e melhorar performance de transformações em larga escala




🧠 O que procuramos:

Mais de 4 anos de experiência em data analytics ou business intelligence
Forte domínio de Python para análise de dados e automação
Bons conhecimentos de SQL e experiência com bases de dados relacionais e analíticas em larga escala
Experiência hands-on com Looker e outros produtos GCP
Bons conhecimentos de data modeling, ETL/ELT e analytics engineering
Experiência com Git
Boa capacidade de comunicação e gestão de stakeholders
Inglês fluente
Espírito curioso e gosto por trabalho em equipa




💡 O que oferecemos:

Projeto desafiante com forte componente analítica e impacto no negócio
Ambiente colaborativo e orientado à melhoria contínua
Oportunidade de trabalhar com stack moderna em dados e cloud
Full visa support (se necessário)




📍 Localização: Híbrido, Norte de Portugal




Pacote Salarial Junior: 12.880€ a 21.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Pacote Salarial Mid: 14.000€ a 28.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Pacote Salarial Senior: 20.000€ a 49.000€ brutos (14 meses) + Subsídio de Alimentação + Benefícios Flexíveis

Description:
🚀 Procuras dar um passo na tua carreira nas áreas de IT e Telecomunicações?

Então chegaste ao sítio certo – à Smart




A tecnologia, a transformação e a inovação fazem parte do nosso ADN.




Somos uma empresa de consultoria tecnológica com mais de uma década de experiência no desenvolvimento de projetos nacionais e internacionais em IT e Telecomunicações e queremos fazer parte da tua história.




🔎 O que precisas para ser um Data Analyst / BI Specialist?

Licenciatura em Engenharia Informática, Tecnologias de Informação, Data Science ou área relacionada
Entre 4 a 6 anos de experiência em análise de dados, business intelligence ou funções similares
Experiência comprovada com Power BI, incluindo criação de dashboards e modelação de dados
Familiaridade com Elasticsearch e execução de queries
Proficiência em Python; conhecimentos básicos de C# são valorizados
Bons conhecimentos de PowerQuery (Power BI)
Forte capacidade analítica e interpretação de datasets complexos
Experiência em ambientes colaborativos e equipas multidisciplinares
Elevada capacidade de resolução de problemas e atenção ao detalhe
Boas competências de comunicação e relacionamento interpessoal




💡 Quais serão as tuas funções?

Desenvolver dashboards interativos e relatórios em Power BI
Modelar, transformar e analisar dados para suporte à decisão
Trabalhar com Elasticsearch para consulta e exploração de dados
Desenvolver scripts em Python para tratamento e automação de dados
Colaborar com diferentes equipas para levantamento de requisitos de negócio
Garantir a qualidade, consistência e fiabilidade dos dados
Identificar tendências, padrões e insights relevantes
Documentar processos e soluções desenvolvidas
Apoiar na otimização de processos de análise e reporting




🌟Porquê juntar-te à nossa equipa?

Tornamos reais os projetos em que participamos.
Valorizamos a qualidade e eficiência no que fazemos.
Somos uma equipa dinâmica, íntegra e de confiança.
Oferecemos ambiente colaborativo e espaço para crescimento profissional.

Porto, hibrido 2x por semana

Title: Data Analyst (HYBRID @Porto)
Description:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!




We are looking for an Amazing: Data Analyst

Seniority: +3 years

Type of position: Hybrid (2x/week in Porto)




The amazing you, will have:

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Statistics or Mathematics;
3+ years of experience as Data Analyst;
Proficiency with ETL, SQL, PowerBI and Alteryx;
Experience with Jaspersoft, VBA, API, Python and Tibco Spotfire are nice to have;
Ability to perform database queries;
Excellent problem-solving and critical-thinking skills;
Strong attention to detail and accuracy;
Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment;
Availability for a hybrid regime at Porto (Portugal);
Fluency in English is mandatory.







To turn yourself from amazing to unique, you´ll have (plus):

Comfort speaking French.




Why should you become part of our family?

You can develop a career that fits you: your career development is personalized, taking in consideration your needs and goals from a short to long term
Interesting Challenges Ahead: you can work for several clients from different sectors of activity
Free training programs: Our training and certification programs in languages, tech, behavior and business will help you to reach your full potential faster
International projects in Benelux: you can gain international experience in Benelux and balance a new way of living with work
Type of projects (depending on the project you might find one of this types of projects):
In hybrid Systems: Is important to balance work with socialization, that´s why a hybrid system works for us and for you
Full Remote projects: If you want to work while you enjoy the comfort of your home
Full Onsite projects: if you prefere the company of your colleagues!
Take care of your well-being: Enjoy our free nutrition, psicologist, general medicine appointments and our yoga and personal training days… all remote!




Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Inovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

HTML:
We’re looking for the special, unique and amazing YOU!

@ KCS IT, we look for the ones that stands out, for those that always wants to be better and fight for it, and for those who has the same values that we do: dedication, energy, integrity, transparency, flexibility, trust, honesty, hard work, proactivity, team work.

At KCS we stand for equality and value diversity. We create a safe, diverse environment where opportunities are equal for everyone! We do not discriminate based on age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender, disability or any factor other than merit. All applications with skills for the position are welcome!


We are looking for an Amazing: Data Analyst

Seniority: +3 years

Type of position: Hybrid (2x/week in Porto)


The amazing you, will have:

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Statistics or Mathematics;
  • 3+ years of experience as Data Analyst;
  • Proficiency with ETL, SQL, PowerBI and Alteryx;
  • Experience with Jaspersoft, VBA, API, Python and Tibco Spotfire are nice to have;
  • Ability to perform database queries;
  • Excellent problem-solving and critical-thinking skills;
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy;
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a team environment;
  • Availability for a hybrid regime at Porto (Portugal);
  • Fluency in English is mandatory.



To turn yourself from amazing to unique, you´ll have (plus):

  • Comfort speaking French.


Why should you become part of our family?

  • You can develop a career that fits you: your career development is personalized, taking in consideration your needs and goals from a short to long term
  • Interesting Challenges Ahead: you can work for several clients from different sectors of activity
  • Free training programs: Our training and certification programs in languages, tech, behavior and business will help you to reach your full potential faster
  • International projects in Benelux: you can gain international experience in Benelux and balance a new way of living with work
  • Type of projects (depending on the project you might find one of this types of projects):
  • In hybrid Systems: Is important to balance work with socialization, that´s why a hybrid system works for us and for you
  • Full Remote projects: If you want to work while you enjoy the comfort of your home
  • Full Onsite projects: if you prefere the company of your colleagues!
  • Take care of your well-being: Enjoy our free nutrition, psicologist, general medicine appointments and our yoga and personal training days… all remote!


Who are we?

Founded in 2008 and based in Lisbon, KCS IT is a consulting company in the field of Information Technology and Services, focused on creating value for our clients through three main areas: Consulting, Outsourcing, Inovation and Training. Our commitment to talent development is unmistakable in the recent opening of the Porto, Leiria and Azores hubs, which aims to develop technology for the national and international market. Since 2018 we have been elected one of the 10 major companies in the “Index of Excellence”, an initiative that aims to reward organizations that invest most in the development and satisfaction of their employees.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
As a global leader in facilities services we connect people and places to make the world work better. Whether directly or indirectly, you’ll play a vital role in supporting our placemakers in delivering exceptional workplace experiences for our customers. Together, we make space for people and businesses to thrive.

Location: UK, Poland

Language: English

Application deadline: 21/02/2026

Main Purpose Of The Position/Key Expectations

We are currently looking for a GKA Analytics Partner to join our Advanced Analytics, Data & AI (ADA) Team. You will act as the ‘vital link and business enabler’ between BI & AI capabilities /solution and it’s business objectives delivering concrete value to the business. You will play a pivotal role in instilling the right data culture in our global key accounts and driving the account objectives within the analytics area. With you onboard, we can drive ISS group wide business progress towards targets, accurate reports for corporate reporting, customers and external benchmarks globally and most importantly, shaping the ISS world through data!

What You’ll Do


Develop Analytics & Data programs – to ensure all stakeholders can effectively interpret and utilize Data for decision-making and performance improvement.
Lead benchmarking projects to generate actionable insights from Data by collaborating closely with the business.
Ensure Data quality and accuracy to support robust benchmarking, KPI assessment, and quarterly management fee at risk reporting.
Own account Master Data.
Develop account Analytics & Data Roadmap.
Develop Data Literacy on the account, at all levels of the organisation.
Develop account Systems Roadmap
Maintain consistent business systems usage and expand adoption in line with the account value proposition
Ensure global alignment and governance in the use of business systems and analytics
Translate the digital technology vision and roadmap into actionable plans in collaboration with client and ISS leadership
Able to manage projects end to end


Who You’ll Work With


Global BI Analysts from across accounts and the wider ADA team.
System owners
Global Account Directors
Operational Performance Lead
Service Leads
Country Leads
Site Leads
Customers (Client facing role)


Key Qualifications


3-5 years of proven experience in Business Intelligence reporting/ Data Analysis required
Experience using Tableau and similar analytics tools desired such as PowerBI
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Experience with collaborating with Global Clients/ International Stakeholders
Must be able to communicate fluently in English
Bachelor’s Degree in Business with Data Analytics/Math/Engineering or another similar field is preferable but not a must
Experience in the Facility Management industry is preferable.


Personal Skills You Excel


Ability to translate business requirements into effective technology and Data solutions
Customer-focused, understanding of site operations and client needs
Proactive and solutions-oriented, with a focus on continuous improvement
Collaborative and inclusive, working effectively with diverse teams across countries and functions
Strong stakeholder management and advisory skills; able to operate at all levels
Strategic and visionary mindset, able to see the big picture


Why ISS

At ISS, we are more than just a service provider of cleaning, food, workplace and technical services, we are a partner in our customers’ success. By creating exceptional service moments and transforming workplaces into spaces where employees feel valued, engaged and productive, we enhance productivity and help our customers to attract talent and grow their businesses. This begins with our own people through training, career development, and a supportive culture – empowering them to deliver outstanding service. We know that when our people thrive, they create spaces where our customers’ employees and businesses thrive too.

ISS is a Place to Be You.

Be who you are. Become what you want. Be part of something bigger.

Become more. Become ISS.

How You’ll Apply

Apply directly via the link on this page by submitting a cover letter, CV and other relevant documents for the position you are applying for.

We look forward to receiving your application as soon as possible.

ISS seeks to BE a place of belonging and CREATE places where every person is welcomed, embraced, and valued for exactly who they are. Places where people feel safe, respected, represented, and supported as their authentic selves.

Title: Business Data Analyst
HTML:
Agradecemos o teu interesse nesta oportunidade! A tua candidatura só será considerada completa após a realização de um assessment digital. No final da candidatura, e caso cumpras os critérios necessários para a função, receberás no email de registo um link para aceder ao mesmo. Esta etapa é obrigatória, já que o nosso processo de recrutamento se baseia na avaliação de competências.


Somos uma empresa de todos e para todos. Com uma base sólida e inclusiva, a MC mantém o foco nas pessoas, para tornar a vida mais simples a clientes, colaboradores ou membros das comunidades. Criamos valor com o contributo de todos, inovamos a cada passo e lideramos de forma próxima, atentos às qualidades e necessidades de cada um. Temos histórias diferentes e ideias diferentes, mas partilhamos o desejo de crescer e de chegar à melhor versão de nós próprios. Tudo o que somos, conseguimos juntos. E não há nada mais inspirador do que isso.


Por isso, se quiseres fazer parte desta equipa e aceitar novos desafios, estamos à tua procura!


Estamos à procura de Business Data Analyst para integrar a nossa equipa de Promo Analytics, em Matosinhos e acreditamos que seremos #betterwithyou!


A tua missão será transformar dados em decisões que realmente fazem a diferença. Vais analisar e interpretar informação sobre promoções, construir modelos que suportem a estratégia comercial e gerar insights claros que ajudem as equipas a tomar decisões mais inteligentes e eficientes, contribuindo para o sucesso do negócio de forma direta e mensurável.


Contamos contigo para…

  • Desenvolver e melhorar modelos de otimização para promoções;
  • Analisar grandes volumes de dados e identificar padrões e oportunidades;
  • Avaliar o desempenho das promoções e gerar recomendações acionáveis;
  • Trabalhar em colaboração com as áreas de negócio, traduzindo dados em decisões práticas;
  • Aplicar técnicas de analytics avançado e metodologias de apoio à decisão;
  • Ajudar a evoluir continuamente as capacidades analíticas da equipa.


Por isso, traz contigo…

  • Formação académica numa área quantitativa (ex.: Matemática, Economia, Engenharia ou similar);
  • 1 a 4 anos de experiência em Data Science, analytics avançado ou áreas similares;
  • Conhecimentos em Python, Spark e SQL (Hive, Impala);
  • Forte capacidade analítica, pensamento crítico e orientação para resultados;
  • Excelentes competências de comunicação e espírito colaborativo;
  • Orientação para o negócio e paixão por transformar dados em impacto real.


O que temos para ti…

  • Subsídio de Alimentação em Cartão Dá;
  • Plano de Telecomunicações com voz, dados e equipamento (a colaboradores efetivos);
  • Programa Flex it Up - Dias Off Extra, Licenças sem Vencimento, Modelo de Trabalho Flexível (quando aplicável);
  • Seguro de Saúde e de Vida (a colaboradores efetivos) com possibilidade de extensão do Seguro de Saúde ao agregado familiar com condições vantajosas;
  • Programa de Benefícios Flexíveis (quando aplicável);
  • Plano de Onboarding e Formação inicial, plataforma de Formação Contínua e Programa de Literacia Financeira;
  • Prémios Escolares e Bolsas de Mérito para filhos de colaboradores (ensino regular e ensino inclusivo), bem como Programas de Férias nas pausas letivas;
  • Kit Nascimento para o bebé que está a chegar;
  • Programas de Mobilidade Interna para a promoção de talento;
  • Vacina contra a Gripe incluindo a sua aplicação (adesão voluntária);
  • Programa Somos Sonae, de apoio psicossocial, financeiro e jurídico aos colaboradores;
  • Sessões de Ergocoaching;
  • Programas de Promoção da Saúde Mental e Consultas de Nutrição;
  • Programa de Descontos e Parcerias em mais de 300 marcas de referência;
  • Disponibilização de café e fruta gratuitamente no local de trabalho
  • Salário Competitivo.


#BETTERTOGETHER #BETTERWITHYOU


Compromisso de D&I MC Sonae:

Trabalhamos para criar um ambiente de trabalho assente na riqueza de perfis e com foco na singularidade, garantindo que todos se sentem respeitados, valorizados pelas suas competências e têm confiança na organização.

Title: Banking Sector | Data Analyst
Description:
At Devoteam, we believe that technology with strong human values can actively drive change for the better. Discover how Tech for People unlocks the future, creating a positive impact on the people and the world around us. We are a global leading player in Digital Transformation for leading organisations across EMEA, with a revenue of €1B. We believe in transforming technology to create value for our clients, partners and employees in a world where technology is developed for people. We are proud of the culture we have built together. We are proud of our people at the service of technology. We are proud of our diverse environment. Because we are #TechforPeople. Join our multidisciplinary team of Cloud experts, Designers, Business consultants, Security experts, Engineers, Developers and other extraordinary talents, spread across more than 20 EMEA countries. Become one of our +10.000 tech and business leaders on cloud, data and cyber security. Let’s fuse creativity with technology together and build innovative solutions that actively change things for the better.

Job Description


Collect, clean, analyze, and interpret data to generate actionable insights that support strategic and operational decisions
Build, maintain, and monitor data pipelines, ensuring reliable extraction, processing, and loading of data
Work closely with end-users and cross-functional teams to ensure data accessibility, usability, and stability
Transform complex information into clear, engaging, and valuable deliverables for business stakeholders
Assess business changes and requirements, evaluating their impact and associated costs
Optimize code, queries, and data processes to improve efficiency and performance
Develop and maintain Airflow pipelines for data extraction, processing, and loading
Optimize Python scripts, SQL queries, and overall data workflows
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities to enhance user experience and anticipate needs
Deliver insights and visual representations that support daily decision-making
Understand business requirements and translate them into precise data solutions



Qualifications


Proven background in data analysis, with experience transforming raw data into actionable insights
Strong expertise in designing and maintaining Airflow pipelines for ETL processes
Proficiency in Python and SQL, with experience in Oracle Database environments and API development
Familiarity with Data Warehouses and large-scale data ecosystems
Experience with Git version control and working in Agile environments
Ability to optimize code, identify data patterns, and produce clear, engaging data outputs
Proficient in English



Additional Information

Devoteam Group works for equal opportunities, promoting its employees based on merit and actively fights against all forms of discrimination. We are convinced that diversity contributes to the creativity, dynamism and excellence of our organization. All of our vacancies are open to people with disabilities.

Title: Internship Data Analyst (m./f./div.)
Description:
Company Description

The Keenfinity Group delivers professional communication and security solutions that connect and protect people and assets. Following its carve-out from the Bosch Group in mid-2025, it operates as independent company within the portfolio of European investment firm Triton. The Group’s four Businesses include Audio delivering professional communication products of the globally renowned brands Bosch, Electro-Voice, Dynacord, RTS and Telex, IQSIGHT Video Systems, Radionix Intrusion & Access, and KEENFINITY Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS). In fiscal year 2025, the group generated revenues of over €1 billion and employed approximately 4,000 people across more than 40 countries.

Job Description

SCOPE OF ROLE & MAIN KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


Collect and combine data from multiple data sources and applications
Support in data cleansing activities
Extract, analyze and provide insights from data to the businesses
Calculate data metrics and KPIs to track data completeness and quality
Define preventive and corrective measures to improve data quality
Perform statistical analysis and create predictive models to automate decision making
Build reports and dashboards to ensure transparency


Qualifications


Academic Degree in management, business informatics, engineering or similar
Data analysis tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
SQL, Python or R programming skills
Knowledge of different file types and how to read them (XML, CSV, JSON, XLSX, TXT, etc.)
Agile methodologies, namely Scrum and Kanban
Project management and documentation tools (Jira, Confluence, or similar)
Excellent communication skills (oral and written) in English
Innovative with continuous improvement mindset
Critical and structured thinking
Meticulous with high attention to detail
Curious about new technologies and strange data patterns


Additional Information

Keenfinity benefits includes:

⚖️ Flexible work conditions

🔀 Hybrid work system

🍽️ Canteen

🅿️ Free parking lot

🏋️ Sports and health related activities (gym)

📚 Training opportunities (i.e., technical training, foreign languages training) & certifications

📈 Opportunities for career progression and continuous professional development

🌐 Exchange with colleagues around the world

💲 Access to great discounts in partnerships and products

🌍All our positions are open to people with disability





At Keenfinity we don’t just build innovative solutions — we shape a smarter, more connected world through technology.

We value different backgrounds, ideas, and experiences and we’re committed to growing, learning, and celebrating success as one team. Everyone is welcome here — we foster an environment where everyone is respected, valued, and encouraged to be their authentic self.

Keenfinity is an equal opportunity employer, offering equal opportunities for all. We welcome applications from people with disabilities and can offer support, if needed. When everyone has a chance to contribute, we all do better.

In fiscal year 2025, the group generated revenues of over €1 billion and employed approximately 4,000 people across more than 40 countries.

Job Description

SCOPE OF ROLE & MAIN KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Collect and combine data from multiple data sources and applications
  • Support in data cleansing activities
  • Extract, analyze and provide insights from data to the businesses
  • Calculate data metrics and KPIs to track data completeness and quality
  • Define preventive and corrective measures to improve data quality
  • Perform statistical analysis and create predictive models to automate decision making
  • Build reports and dashboards to ensure transparency

Qualifications

  • Academic Degree in management, business informatics, engineering or similar
  • Data analysis tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
  • SQL, Python or R programming skills
  • Knowledge of different file types and how to read them (XML, CSV, JSON, XLSX, TXT, etc.)
  • Agile methodologies, namely Scrum and Kanban
  • Project management and documentation tools (Jira, Confluence, or similar)
  • Excellent communication skills (oral and written) in English
  • Innovative with continuous improvement mindset
  • Critical and structured thinking
  • Meticulous with high attention to detail
  • Curious about new technologies and strange data patterns

Additional Information

Keenfinity benefits includes:

⚖️ Flexible work conditions

🔀 Hybrid work system

🍽️ Canteen

🅿️ Free parking lot

🏋️ Sports and health related activities (gym)

📚 Training opportunities (i.e., technical training, foreign languages training) & certifications

📈 Opportunities for career progression and continuous professional development

🌐 Exchange with colleagues around the world

💲 Access to great discounts in partnerships and products

🌍All our positions are open to people with disability



    • At Keenfinity we don’t just build innovative solutions — we shape a smarter, more connected world through technology.

    We value different backgrounds, ideas, and experiences and we’re committed to growing, learning, and celebrating success as one team. Everyone is welcome here — we foster an environment where everyone is respected, valued, and encouraged to be their authentic self.

    Keenfinity is an equal opportunity employer, offering equal opportunities for all. We welcome applications from people with disabilities and can offer support, if needed. When everyone has a chance to contribute, we all do better.

Title: Senior Data Analyst - MarAnalytics
Description:
Farfetch is a leading global marketplace for the luxury fashion industry, connecting customers in over 190 countries with items from more than 50 countries and over 1,400 of the world’s best brands and charming boutiques from around the world. Farfetch opens a world of luxury for endless expressions of style, delivering a truly unique shopping experience and access to the most extensive selection of luxury on a global marketplace.

MARKETING
The Marketing team is data-driven and results-oriented, focused on achieving measurable outcomes across all digital advertising and marketing channels. Growth is a key performance indicator, and they constantly optimize for return on ad spend.

PORTO
Our office is near Porto, in the north of Portugal, and is located in a vibrant business hub. It offers a dynamic and welcoming environment where our employees can connect and network with a large community of tech professionals.

THE ROLE
This is an exciting role for a skilled and experienced Senior Data Analyst to drive data-driven decision-making across the organization. This role will focus on experimentation, measurement, and delivering actionable insights to optimize performance and enhance business outcomes.

What You'll Do


Design, execute, and optimize experiments across paid marketing channels, leveraging data-driven methodologies to increase ROAS.
Continuously refine experimentation frameworks to identify growth opportunities and enhance performance.
Proactively identify opportunities to automate reporting processes, improve efficiency, and reduce manual effort.
Integrate and analyse data from diverse internal and external sources to uncover acquisition, retention, and optimization opportunities for paid marketing channels.
Provide actionable recommendations to improve campaign effectiveness.
Effectively communicate insights, analyses, and recommendations to stakeholders at all levels, from marketing teams to senior leadership.
Build and implement statistical models to forecast demand, measure sales incrementality, and assess the impact of key marketing initiatives.
Leverage advanced analytical techniques to enhance decision-making and drive strategy.
Stay updated on industry trends, new analytical tools, and marketing technologies.
Apply this knowledge to improve existing processes, adopt innovative solutions, and maintain a competitive edge





Who Are You


A professional with 3+ years of experience in a data role, preferably in an internet company;
Strong analytical and quantitative skills; experience using data and metrics to test theories, confirm assumptions, and measure success;
Expert in SQL. BigQuery experience is a plus;
Familiar with data visualisation tools such as Looker, Tableau, Google Analytics, Data Studio, PowerBI or Microstrategy;
Degree educated. A quantitative field is considered a bonus;
Advanced Excel knowledge;
Advanced in English, both written and oral;
Excellent written and oral communication;
Using R or Python for statistical modelling is a plus;
Experience in Digital Marketing and Retention Analytics is a plus;
Curious about learning and applying data science to solve business problems





Rewards & Benefits


Health insurance for the whole family, flexible working environment and well-being support and tools
Extra days off, sabbatical program and days for you to give back for the community
Training opportunities and free access to Udemy
Flexible benefits program
Flexible work environment- Hybrid Model (3 days a week from the office, 2 days from home)





EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT & SCAM DISCLAIMER


EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES STATEMENT- Farfetch is an equal opportunities employer ensuring that all applicants are treated equally and fairly throughout our recruitment process. We are determined that no applicant experiences discrimination on the basis of sex, race, ethnicity, religion or belief, disability, age, gender identity, ancestry, sexual orientation, veteran status, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.
SCAM DISCLAIMER- It has come to our attention that there may be fraudulent activities involving individuals or organizations falsely claiming to represent Farfetch in order to attract candidates to a SCAM. Please be aware that Farfetch does not conduct recruitment processes through messaging apps or any unofficial communication channels, other than our official careers website. Additionally, Farfetch will never ask candidates for any form of payment during the recruitment process





We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Analyst and communication expert (m/f/div.)
Description:
Company Description

The Keenfinity Group delivers professional communication and security solutions that connect and protect people and assets. Following its carve-out from the Bosch Group in mid-2025, it operates as independent company within the portfolio of European investment firm Triton. The Group’s four Businesses include Audio delivering professional communication products of the globally renowned brands Bosch, Electro-Voice, Dynacord, RTS and Telex, IQSIGHT Video Systems, Radionix Intrusion & Access, and KEENFINITY Electronics Manufacturing Services (EMS). In fiscal year 2025, the group generated revenues of over €1 billion and employed approximately 4,000 people across more than 40 countries.

Job Description

We are professionalizing our end‑to‑end finance processes, harmonizing SAP templates and establishing a global BPO model. In this role, you will actively shape how our future accounting landscape looks.

This hybrid role combines advanced data analytics with communication excellence. You will lead the development of modern Power BI dashboards for Finance (I2P, B2C, B2R processes) and drive internal communication that strengthens transparency, alignment, and engagement across the global organization.

Your Responsibilities


Power BI & Finance Analytics
Drive the Power BI reporting initiative for Finance with focus on I2P, B2C, B2R.
Integrate data from SAP, Comarch ECM, and additional systems.
Build, automate, and maintain high‑quality dashboards and KPI landscapes.
Ensure data quality through validation, modeling, structuring, and governance.
Support the global BPO setup with data-driven insights and reporting automation.
Conduct ad-hoc analyses for Finance, Operations, and Management.
Support new BI technologies, datasets, APIs, and automation tools (Power Automate).
Organizational Communication & Stakeholder Enablement
Create clear, engaging communication about Finance organization status and improvements.
Translate complex processes into easy-to-understand materials (presentations, infographics, intranet pages, newsletters, LinkedIn posts if appropriate).
Design communication around new tools, processes, KPIs, and success stories.
Structure and maintain escalation paths and process explanations.
Prepare steering committee materials, leadership briefings, and decision papers.
Support workshop moderation (onsite and remote) to align global stakeholders.
Ensure consistent corporate messaging across M365, Intranet, SharePoint, and other channels.


Why this role is attractive


High visibility: your insights feed directly into management decisions.
Greenfield reporting landscape: build modern BI from the ground up.
International collaboration and true KPI ownership.
Opportunity to drive automation (e.g., Power BI/Power Automate).


Qualifications


Degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, Engineering or related field.
2+ years of experience in Data Analytics, BI, or reporting.
Strong skills in Power BI, DAX, Power Query, and data modeling.Hands-on experience with SAP or SAP-based data models; experience with ECM (e.g. Comarch) is a plus.
Excellent English skills; ability to communicate complex topics simply and visually.
Structured, analytical, solution-oriented mindset with good storytelling capabilities.Excellent English skills.


Nice to have


Experience with end‑to‑end finance processes (I2P, P2P, O2C, B2C, R2R/B2R).
Skills in SQL, Python, R, or data automation tooling.
Knowledge of Data Vault, star schema, or modern data architecture.
Experience with M365 communication (SharePoint, Viva Engage, Teams channels, etc.).
Basic design skills (PowerPoint, Canva, Figma) or workshop facilitation experience.


Legal Notes


GDPR‑compliant handling of personal data; collection limited to job‑relevant information.
No collection of irrelevant private information in line with Portuguese data‑protection rules.
Fully non‑discriminatory posting in accordance with EU and Portuguese labor law.


Additional Information

Keenfinity Benefits includes:

⚖️ Flexible work conditions

🔀 Hybrid work system (2 - 3 days at the office)

🧑‍⚕️ Health insurance and medical office on site (nutrition, psychology, physiotherapy and general clinic)

🍽️ Canteen

🅿️ Free parking lot

🏋️ Sports and health related activities (gym)

📚 Training opportunities (i.e., technical training, foreign languages training) & certifications

📈 Opportunities for career progression and continuous professional development

🌐 Exchange with colleagues around the world

💲 Access to great discounts in partnerships and products

🌍All our positions are open to people with disability

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst
Description:
HBK is a leading organization in the testing and measurement industry that is on a journey to ensure it leverages data-driven insights to make strategic business decisions. We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled Business Intelligence Analyst to join our dynamic team. The successful candidate will play a pivotal role in enhancing our data analytics and reporting capabilities. The team is currently rebuilding our data landscape, and this is a real opportunity to drive change within the business area.

Job Summary:

As a Business Intelligence Analyst, you will play a key role in gathering, analyzing, and transforming data into actionable insights that drive informed decision-making at HBK. Drawing upon your extensive expertise in Business Intelligence, you will collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver advanced data solutions and enhance operational efficiency.

Key Responsibilities:


Data Strategy: Develop and execute a comprehensive data strategy that leverages our data assets to meet the organization’s analytical needs and objectives.
Advanced Analytics: Apply advanced statistical and predictive analytics techniques to uncover trends, patterns, and insights in data, enabling more informed decision-making.
Reporting Excellence: Lead the design and maintenance of sophisticated, interactive reports and dashboards using modern business intelligence and data visualization platforms (SAP Analytics Cloud, Power BI, or similar tools). Create highly visual and intuitive data visualizations for use by business stakeholders.
Data Integration and Governance: Oversee the integration of data from diverse sources, ensuring data quality, consistency, and adherence to data governance policies. Collaborate with IT and data engineering teams to optimize data pipelines.
Performance Optimization: Continuously enhance the performance of our systems, including optimizing data processing and reporting capabilities.
Business Requirements Analysis: Work closely with business leaders to understand their analytical needs and translate these requirements into actionable data solutions.
Data Modeling Expertise: Design and implement complex data models to facilitate advanced reporting and analytics, encompassing both logical and physical data models.
Data Security and Compliance: Ensure strict data security and compliance with internal policies and industry regulations throughout all data handling and reporting activities.
Mentorship and Training: Provide guidance and training to other analysts and end-users, fostering self-service analytics adoption.


Qualifications:


Bachelor's degree in Business, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field (Master's degree preferred).
A minimum of 3 years of experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst.
In-depth expertise in data warehousing, ETL processes, data modeling, and advanced analytics techniques.
Proficiency in SQL and familiarity with data visualization tools.
Exceptional problem-solving skills and analytical thinking.
Strong communication and presentation skills.
Proven ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
Knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in business intelligence and data analytics.


Additional Information:


Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.
Opportunities for career growth and professional development within our organization.
A collaborative and innovative work environment equipped with state-of-the-art data analytics tools.


If you are a seasoned professional with a passion for driving advanced data-driven decision-making, we invite you to apply and become a pivotal part of our team at HBK. Join us in shaping the future of data analytics for our business's success.

We Offer

Freedom with responsibility is the framework for HBK’s employees. This allows for a good balance between work and family life and for constant development of professional and personal skills in an international and enjoyable working environment.

One Company – HBK

On 1 January 2019, Brüel & Kjær and HBM merged their activities into a new company called HBK (Hottinger Brüel & Kjær). HBK is a subsidiary of Spectris Limited, and employees 3,500 employees worldwide and production facilities in Denmark, Germany, the UK, the USA, China and Portugal and presence in 80 countries, HBK is a leading player in the test and measurement area. For further info please visit www.hbkworld.com.

  • Data Integration and Governance: Oversee the integration of data from diverse sources, ensuring data quality, consistency, and adherence to data governance policies. Collaborate with IT and data engineering teams to optimize data pipelines.
  • Performance Optimization: Continuously enhance the performance of our systems, including optimizing data processing and reporting capabilities.
  • Business Requirements Analysis: Work closely with business leaders to understand their analytical needs and translate these requirements into actionable data solutions.
  • Data Modeling Expertise: Design and implement complex data models to facilitate advanced reporting and analytics, encompassing both logical and physical data models.
  • Data Security and Compliance: Ensure strict data security and compliance with internal policies and industry regulations throughout all data handling and reporting activities.
  • Mentorship and Training: Provide guidance and training to other analysts and end-users, fostering self-service analytics adoption.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in Business, Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field (Master's degree preferred).
  • A minimum of 3 years of experience as a Business Intelligence Analyst.
  • In-depth expertise in data warehousing, ETL processes, data modeling, and advanced analytics techniques.
  • Proficiency in SQL and familiarity with data visualization tools.
  • Exceptional problem-solving skills and analytical thinking.
  • Strong communication and presentation skills.
  • Proven ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
  • Knowledge of industry best practices and emerging trends in business intelligence and data analytics.

Additional Information:

  • Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package.
  • Opportunities for career growth and professional development within our organization.
  • A collaborative and innovative work environment equipped with state-of-the-art data analytics tools.

If you are a seasoned professional with a passion for driving advanced data-driven decision-making, we invite you to apply and become a pivotal part of our team at HBK. Join us in shaping the future of data analytics for our business's success.

We Offer

Freedom with responsibility is the framework for HBK’s employees. This allows for a good balance between work and family life and for constant development of professional and personal skills in an international and enjoyable working environment.

One Company – HBK

On 1 January 2019, Brüel & Kjær and HBM merged their activities into a new company called HBK (Hottinger Brüel & Kjær). HBK is a subsidiary of Spectris Limited, and employees 3,500 employees worldwide and production facilities in Denmark, Germany, the UK, the USA, China and Portugal and presence in 80 countries, HBK is a leading player in the test and measurement area. For further info please visit www.hbkworld.com.

Title: PowerBI Analyst & Administrator
Description:
Kantar is the world’s leading AI-native marketing data and analytics business and an indispensable brand partner to the world’s top companies. We combine the most meaningful attitudinal and behavioural data with deep expertise and advanced analytics to uncover how people think and act.




We help clients understand what has happened and why and how to shape the marketing strategies that shape their future.




About the job




This hybrid role combines the analytical expertise of a Power BI Report Analyst with the technical oversight of a Power BI Administrator. The individual will be responsible for designing insightful dashboards and reports while also managing and optimizing the Power BI environment. This role supports business decision-making, ensures secure and scalable BI operations, and enforces governance and compliance standards!




Job Goals




Design and develop Power BI dashboards and reports for internal and external use.
Collaborate with business units to gather requirements and translate them into visual analytics.
Maintain and verify data accuracy across reports and visualizations.
Analyze datasets to identify performance trends, risks, and opportunities.
Present insights in written and verbal formats to managers and executives.
Monitor data pipelines and ensure timely refreshes and updates.
Administer Power BI tenant settings, workspace access, and user permissions.
Configure and maintain data gateways for on-premises and cloud data sources.
Monitor usage, performance, and capacity to optimize resources.
Implement governance policies, security protocols, and compliance standards.
Support deployment pipelines and lifecycle management of reports and dashboards.
Automate administrative tasks using PowerShell or REST APIs.
Troubleshoot issues related to data refreshes, report failures, and user access.
Maintain documentation and best practices for Power BI operations.




Ideal Skills & Capabilities




4-6 years of experience in business intelligence, data analysis, or Power BI administration.
Proficiency in Power BI, DAX, Power Query, and SQL.
Strong analytical and visualization skills.
Experience with data modeling and ETL processes.
Excellent communication and storytelling abilities.
Deep understanding of Power BI service architecture, licensing, and capacity planning.
Experience with PowerShell scripting and Microsoft 365/Azure AD integration.
Strong problem-solving and troubleshooting skills.
Familiarity with Excel, Tableau, or other BI tools is a plus.




Preferred Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field.
Microsoft Certified: Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) or Power BI Administrator certification.
Experience with cloud data sources (Azure, AWS Redshift).
Experience with Power BI Premium and Embedded capacities.
Understanding of data governance and compliance.
Knowledge of audit logs, usage metrics, and performance tuning.




What’s in it for you




On top of all your normal benefits like holiday, meal cards and life assurance, we also offer:

Five days of Life Leave per year; for those days when life just…gets in the way.
Birthday leave – one extra day of holiday taken on the nearest workday to your birthday
Two days a year to give back and volunteer with an organisation of your choice.
Enhanced primary and secondary carer leave for however you choose to grow your family.
The chance to hit the beach on your lunch break (yes, we have somewhere you can store your surfboard!)




Please be aware, the majority of our roles are hybrid, working three days a week in our office.




We’re not able to offer visa sponsorship or help with relocation support for this role. Please make sure you’ve got the right to work in the country where this role is located before applying.




What part of Kantar might I be joining?




You’ll be joining our Profiles division, home to specialists in survey design, sampling and data science. With the world’s largest audience network (over 170 million people), we’re trusted by many of the worlds leading brands to provide amazing insights from real people.




Why join Kantar?




We shape the brands of tomorrow by better understanding people everywhere. By understanding people, we can understand what drives their decisions, actions, and aspirations on a global scale. And if we combine the expertise of our people with the latest AI technology, we can really help brands discover some amazing insights.




And because we know people, we like to make sure our own people are being looked after as well. Equality of opportunity for everyone is our highest priority and we support our colleagues to work in a way works for them. We encourage applications from all backgrounds and sections of society. Even if you feel like you’re not an exact match, we’d love to receive your application and talk to you about this job or others at Kantar.

Title: Senior Data Analyst | PowerBI / Python
Description:
We are growing. And we are looking for talented people.




At Decskill, we believe that technological excellence is driven by human talent.

We are an IT consulting company with more than 10 years of consolidated experience in the market, focused on building long-term relationships with both our clients and our people. Today, we are a community of over 800 professionals, working from Lisbon, Porto, and Madrid, contributing to impactful technology initiatives.

As part of the Astek Group, we combine a strong local culture with a global presence, being active in around 23 countries across 4 continents. This allows us to offer an international context, diverse challenges, and long-term career opportunities, while staying close to our teams.




We would like to meet a Senior Data Analyst | PowerBI / Python for a hybrid project based in Porto.




Requirements:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Science (or related field);
6+ years of experience in Data Analysis / Business Intelligence roles;
Strong experience with Power BI (dashboards + data modeling);
Experience with Elasticsearch (management + querying);
Strong proficiency in Python (C# is a plus);
Advanced knowledge of PowerQuery (Power BI);
Strong ability to work with and interpret complex datasets;
Fluency in English (spoken and written) is mandatory;
Strong problem-solving mindset and attention to detail;
Excellent communication and stakeholder management;
Ability to work effectively in a cross-functional, collaborative environment.







Responsibilities:

Manage and maintain Elasticsearch databases, ensuring data accuracy, availability, and performance
Design and develop Power BI dashboards that deliver clear, actionable business insights
Handle daily data integration requests, including troubleshooting and issue resolution
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (business analysts, technical teams, support) to ensure end-to-end data alignment
Analyze complex datasets to generate insights and recommendations that drive business decisions
Develop Python-based tools and scripts to improve data processing and automation workflows
Create documentation and training materials on data tools and best practices




Are you looking for an environment that values curiosity and commitment? Here, your contribution has real impact and individual growth is taken seriously.




Find with us the right opportunity to grow!




What you can expect from us

· Long-term projects with national and international context.

· Opportunities to grow technically and professionally.

· A people-first culture, focused on transparency and trust.

· Teams that value ownership, collaboration and stability.




Join us Send us your CV in English to mariana.azevedo@decskill.com with reference MA/DAPT.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
We are Körber -- an international technology group

With about 13,000 employees, more than 100 locations worldwide and a common goal:

We turn entrepreneurial thinking into customer success and shape the technological change.

In the Business Areas Pharma, Supply Chain and Technologies, we offer products, solutions and services that inspire. We act fast to customer needs, execute ideas seamlessly, and with our innovations, we create added value for our customers. In doing so, we are increasingly building on ecosystems that solve the challenges of today and tomorrow.

At Körber Porto we provide state-of-the-art global IT services and offer expertise in cloud architecture, cybersecurity, deep tech, and AI -- making us a key driver of the Group's digital transformation.

We are data scientists, engineers, IT specialists, designers, product experts, and venture builders. We are a team, united by the shared ambition to exploit the value of data, leverage the advantage of corporate power and a startup's proficiency with new technologies to develop industry-leading software solutions and build innovative digital tech ventures that transform global manufacturing.

Senior Data Analyst (m/f/d)

Your role in out team

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will take ownership of Körber’s marketing analytics reporting landscape, with a strong focus on Power BI and stakeholder-facing dashboards. You will be responsible for managing the Power BI workspace, maintaining and improving reporting standards, and ensuring business users have reliable and actionable insights across marketing, website, and CRM data. Working as part of the data team, you will play a central role in translating data into decisions across the marketing organization. Your focus will be on delivering high-quality reporting, answering ad hoc analytical questions, and driving continuous improvement in analytics for stakeholders. Our tech stack includes Power BI, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, and a central data lake.

Responsibilities



Own and manage the Power BI workspace for marketing, ensuring dashboards and reports are accurate, scalable, and user-friendly.
Develop, maintain, and optimize Power BI dashboards for key marketing stakeholders across campaign, website, and CRM performance.
Act as a point of contact for analytics topics within marketing, translating business questions into clear analyses and actionable insights.
Analyze marketing, web, and CRM data to identify trends, opportunities, and areas for improvement.
Collaborate closely within the data team to ensure reporting is based on reliable and well-structured data.
Identify gaps in available data and define requirements for new data sources or improvements to existing datasets.
Drive best practices for reporting design, KPI definitions, documentation, and workspace governance within Power BI.
Support ad hoc requests from stakeholders with clear analysis, visualizations, and recommendations.
Present findings to technical and non-technical audiences in a clear and business-relevant way.



Your Profile



Strong experience with Power BI, including dashboard development, data modeling, workspace management, and report optimization.
Strong SQL skills and confidence working with structured data in modern cloud-based data environments.
Solid understanding of marketing analytics, including website, campaign, and CRM data.
Ability to translate stakeholder needs into effective dashboards, KPIs, and analytical outputs.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a proactive and ownership-driven mindset.
Experience working closely with cross-functional stakeholders in a business-facing analytics role.
Excellent communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to explain complex findings in a simple way.
Familiarity with Microsoft Fabric, Databricks, and data lake environments is a plus.

Title: Market Data Compliance Analyst
Description:
Company Description

dxFeed is a leading provider of data services for the capital markets industry. The company sources and stores direct market data feeds from various exchanges and market participants worldwide. dxFeed has built one of the most comprehensive ticker plants, offering the broadest range of data services for streaming, consolidation, storage, extraction, and analytics. These include index construction and maintenance for buy-side and sell-side institutions of the global financial industry.

Job Description

We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Market Data Compliance Analyst. The successful candidate will work closely with the Market Data Compliance Manager to ensure overall compliance with Exchange regulations. This role involves performing daily compliance-related tasks, including communication with Exchanges, clients, and partners, conducting end-user status verification, managing usage reporting, and supporting internal teams through research and documentation.

We Expect The Market Data Compliance Analyst To


Assist in ensuring compliance with Exchange market data regulations and licensing requirements,
Maintain a market data inventory (licenses, customers),
Perform end-user status verification (professional vs. non-professional) to ensure accurate classification,
Prepare monthly usage reports and verify data for accuracy and compliance,
Serve as a point of contact for communication with Exchanges, clients, and internal partners on compliance matters,
Assist with various internal and external compliance-related inquiries,
Research, document, and analyze compliance topics, regulatory changes, and market data policies,
Collaborate with internal teams, including IT and Development, to define and communicate requirements for compliance tools such as reporting systems and user management platforms,
Support the development and improvement of internal compliance processes and tools,
Assist with audit preparation and ensure compliance documentation is accurate and current.


Qualifications

Must-have skills:


Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, Law, or a related field preferred,
Advanced level of English (clear, accurate written and verbal communication skills),
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with clients, partners, and teams.
High attention to detail and strong analytical capabilities,
Proficiency in MS Office Suite (especially Excel) and comfort working with databases or compliance platforms,
Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, and work both independently and as part of a team.


Nice-to-have Skills


Prior experience in market data compliance, financial services compliance, or related roles is a strong advantage.
Experience collaborating with technical teams to define business requirements is a plus,
Experience with Confluence and Jira is a plus.


Additional Information

Care for the employees is one of dxFeed's core values. For the suggested position, we offer a benefits package that will guarantee the comfort of our new teammate.

Work Regime Flexibility Benefits


Possibility of hybrid/remote work mode,
Flexible working hours.


Health And Recreation Benefits


Fully paid additional wellness days (3 unwell days per year),
Medical insurance for the employees and children,
Reimbursement of fitness / Urban Sports Club Membership,
Meal allowance (Coverflex Card),
Flexpay system (Coverflex).


Facility Benefits


Modern office with new equipment,
Free drinks and snacks.


Community Benefits


Free admission to corporate external events,
Possibility of joining conferences and professional fairs,
Personal branding development support.


Professional Training Benefits


Local language courses for foreign employees,
Unlimited access to self-learning platforms,


Social Benefits


Parental bonus,
Pension plan (Coverflex),
Referral bonus,
Blood donation paid leave.

Title: Data Science Associate (Fashion)
Description:
Who We Are

We are Metyis, a forward-thinking, global company that develops and delivers solutions around Big Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing and Design and provides Advisory services. We have offices in 15 locations with a talent pool of 1000+ employees and more than 50 nationalities, dedicated to creating long-lasting impact and growth for our business partners and clients.

Together with HUGO BOSS, our esteemed business partner, we have embarked on a joint venture and created the HUGO BOSS Digital Campus, dedicated to increasing the data analytics, eCommerce and technology capabilities of the company and boosting digital sales. The HUGO BOSS Digital Campus employees will help create a state-of-the-art data architecture infrastructure, advanced business analytics, and the development and enhancement of HUGO BOSS’ eCommerce platform and services.

This collaborative environment will provide the capabilities required for HUGO BOSS to maximise data usage and support its growth trajectory towards becoming the leading premium tech-driven fashion platform worldwide.

We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.

What We Offer


Opportunity to accelerate the pace of digitalization through advanced technology, business intelligence, and analytics.
Driving high-impact insights enhancing decision making across the entire organization.
Interaction with senior business leaders on regular basis to drive their business towards impactful change.
Become part of a fast-growing international and diverse team.



What You Will Do


Conduct advanced data analysis focused on Omnichannel Customer Behavior, CRM performance, and sales effectiveness across digital and physical touchpoints (specific for our team).
Translate business problems into analytical solutions, from requirement gathering to model deployment and monitoring.
Build, deploy, and maintain machine learning models (e.g. Churn, CLV, Segmentation, Personalization) using modern MLOps frameworks (e.g. MLflow, Git).
Leverage Databricks, Azure, or similar cloud infrastructure for scalable data processing and model deployment.
Use Python, PySpark, and common ML libraries (e.g. scikit-learn, XGBoost, LightGBM, etc.) for data transformation, model development, and evaluation.
Apply statistical methods (e.g. hypothesis testing, A/B testing, confidence intervals) to validate performance and derive actionable insights.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly through compelling storytelling and visualizations.
Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders (e.g. Business teams, Data Engineering, IT teams) and contribute to customer-centric strategies.
Support team development through knowledge sharing, collaboration, and participation in agile working models.



What You Will Bring

Solid programming skills in Python, including libraries like pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, matplotlib, seaborn, and PySpark.
Experience with Azure (preferred) or AWS, especially in the context of Databricks, data pipelines, and model hosting.
Good understanding of data warehousing and querying using SQL.
Familiarity with statistical concepts (e.g. experiment design, hypothesis testing, p-values).
Experience or strong interest in Generative AI applications, such as LLM-based solutions, prompt engineering, RAG pipelines, or AI-assisted analytics is a plus.
Exposure to Reinforcement Learning concepts is a plus, especially in areas like decision optimization, personalization, or next-best-action use cases.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, including the ability to present technical insights to non-technical audiences.
Fluency in English (written and spoken).
Experience within the retail and fashion industry (nice to have).
Experience within international environments, consultancies, or a start-up environment (nice to have).

At Metyis, we want to welcome and retain all talents, regardless of gender, age, origin or sexual orientation, and irrespective of whether or not they are living with a disability, as each of them has their own experience and identity.

Title: Market Data Compliance Analyst
Description:
dxFeed is a leading provider of data services for the capital markets industry. The company sources and stores direct market data feeds from various exchanges and market participants worldwide. dxFeed has built one of the most comprehensive ticker plants, offering the broadest range of data services for streaming, consolidation, storage, extraction, and analytics. These include index construction and maintenance for buy-side and sell-side institutions of the global financial industry.




Job Description




We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Market Data Compliance Analyst. The successful candidate will work closely with the Market Data Compliance Manager to ensure overall compliance with Exchange regulations. This role involves performing daily compliance-related tasks, including communication with Exchanges, clients, and partners, conducting end-user status verification, managing usage reporting, and supporting internal teams through research and documentation.




We expect the Market Data Compliance Analyst to:

Assist in ensuring compliance with Exchange market data regulations and licensing requirements,
Maintain a market data inventory (licenses, customers),
Perform end-user status verification (professional vs. non-professional) to ensure accurate classification,
Prepare monthly usage reports and verify data for accuracy and compliance,
Serve as a point of contact for communication with Exchanges, clients, and internal partners on compliance matters,
Assist with various internal and external compliance-related inquiries,
Research, document, and analyze compliance topics, regulatory changes, and market data policies,
Collaborate with internal teams, including IT and Development, to define and communicate requirements for compliance tools such as reporting systems and user management platforms,
Support the development and improvement of internal compliance processes and tools,
Assist with audit preparation and ensure compliance documentation is accurate and current.




Qualifications




Must-have skills:

Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, Law, or a related field preferred,
Advanced level of English (clear, accurate written and verbal communication skills),
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with clients, partners, and teams.
High attention to detail and strong analytical capabilities,
Proficiency in MS Office Suite (especially Excel) and comfort working with databases or compliance platforms,
Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, and work both independently and as part of a team.




Nice-to-have skills:

Prior experience in market data compliance, financial services compliance, or related roles is a strong advantage.
Experience collaborating with technical teams to define business requirements is a plus,
Experience with Confluence and Jira is a plus.




Additional Information




Care for the employees is one of dxFeed's core values. For the suggested position, we offer a benefits package that will guarantee the comfort of our new teammate.




Work Regime Flexibility benefits:

Possibility of hybrid/remote work mode,
Flexible working hours.




Health and recreation benefits:

Fully paid additional wellness days (3 unwell days per year),
Medical insurance for the employees and children,
Reimbursement of fitness / Urban Sports Club Membership,
Meal allowance (Coverflex Card),
Flexpay system (Coverflex).




Facility benefits:

Modern office with new equipment,
Free drinks and snacks.




Community benefits:

Free admission to corporate external events,
Possibility of joining conferences and professional fairs,
Personal branding development support.




Professional training benefits:

Local language courses for foreign employees,
Unlimited access to self-learning platforms,




Social benefits:

Parental bonus,
Pension plan (Coverflex),
Referral bonus,
Blood donation paid leave.

Title: Procurement Data and Insight Analyst
Description:
Description

The Procurement Data and Insight Analyst plays a key role in supporting Unilabs’ procurement operations through data analysis, reporting, and actionable insights. This role requires a strong understanding of procurement processes, data analysis methods, and business intelligence tools. The successful candidate will work closely with the procurement team to optimize strategies, identify savings opportunities, and monitor supplier performance.




About the role

The Procurement Data and Insight Analyst is responsible for building and maintaining analytical tools and intelligence systems that support procurement planning and operations across Europe. This is primarily a technical role focused on dashboards, automated reporting, and monitoring frameworks that help the procurement team act proactively on contract risks, savings opportunities, and process bottlenecks.

The successful candidate will work at the intersection of data engineering, procurement strategy, and operational excellence, translating complex spend data into clear, actionable insights for senior stakeholders.




Key responsibilities

Tool development & BI ownership core focus

Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards and automated reports to support procurement planning and decision-making
Develop and manage monitoring frameworks that generate real-time alerts for contract expirations, process delays, and sourcing gaps
Build and maintain Excel-based tools using automation features such as Power Query or VBA for ad hoc analysis and modelling
Ensure data quality, refresh reliability, and clear documentation across all developed tools




Procurement intelligence & monitoring core focus

Identify spend categories not covered by active contracts and prioritize them for negotiation cycles
Maintain a live view of the contract portfolio, including renewal timelines, savings tracking, and compliance status
Flag delayed procurement processes and escalate them to the relevant stakeholders in a timely manner
Produce regular spend analyses to identify trends, maverick buying, and cost-saving opportunities across categories




Data analysis & reporting

Collect, structure, and interpret large procurement datasets to identify patterns and improvement opportunities
Define and maintain KPIs, SLAs, and performance benchmarks across the procurement function
Provide ad hoc analysis and reporting to support data-driven decisions by the leadership team




Supplier performance & strategy

Monitor supplier performance against defined KPIs and SLAs, identify gaps, and recommend corrective actions
Conduct market research and supplier analysis to support category strategies and sourcing decisions
Collaborate with cross-functional teams, including finance, operations, and legal, to align procurement insights with business priorities




Process improvement

Identify opportunities to streamline procurement workflows and improve operational efficiency
Support the implementation of process standardization initiatives across markets

Requirements




Education

Bachelor's degree in Business, Supply Chain Management, Finance, Data Analytics, or a related field




Experience

3–5 years of experience in procurement, data analysis, or business intelligence roles
Proven experience building and owning dashboards and reporting tools; consumption-only experience is not sufficient
Experience in the healthcare, pharmaceutical, or diagnostics industry is a strong advantage




Technical skills

Hands-on experience developing in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and Power Query, is required
Advanced Excel skills, including Power Query and VBA automation, are required
Experience with Tableau or equivalent BI tools is a plus
Experience with Coupa or similar procurement/P2P platforms (e.g., SAP Ariba, Jaggaer) is required
Ability to build automated reporting pipelines and alert mechanisms from structured procurement data
Familiarity with SQL or Python for data extraction and transformation is a plus




Skills & competencies

Strong analytical and structured problem-solving skills, with the ability to work with large and complex datasets
Clear and direct communication skills, with the ability to translate data into executive-level insights
High attention to detail, strong organizational skills, and the ability to manage competing priorities
Collaborative and cross-functional mindset, with the ability to work effectively across finance, operations, and legal teams




Languages

Fluency in English is mandatory, as it is the working language of the team
Proficiency in Portuguese, French, Spanish, or another European language is a strong advantage given Unilabs’ pan-European footprint

Title: HR Data Analyst
Description:
stow is one of the leading suppliers of storage solutions. Our core activity is the development, manufacturing and installation of top-quality racking systems for the storage of palletized or small goods and longer items, as well as mezzanine structures.





Job Description



As an HR Data Analyst based in Ovar, Portugal, and reporting to the Group’s International HR Director based in Belgium, you will gather data from various HR systems and reports, develop and maintain data models and respective dashboards for HR metrics, as well as provide data-driven insights to support strategic decision-making about HR topics such as salary transparency, gender equality, etc.



Main responsibilities:



Gather data from various sources, namely several HR systems, platforms and reports
Clean, transform, and validate data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and data integrity
Analyse HR data and metrics – such as turnover rates, employee satisfaction, absenteeism, demographics, etc. – generate reports and dashboards for senior leadership
Collaborate with HR and business leaders to identify analytics projects
Evaluate HR systems and reports, and recommend process improvements to enhance efficiency


Qualifications


Minimum 3 years’ experience in a similar HR role (HR background)
Proficient in data analysis tools, namely MS Excel and Power BI
English fluent, both verbal and written
Solid communication skills, able to collaborate with several stakeholders in a team environment
Analytical mindset with a proactive attitude, able to act in a fast-moving environment
Available for short business trips to Belgium and other stow Group locations


Additional Information


Work closely together with other rack stars in an open and enthusiastic international environment
